title: Meredith Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)

infobox.value: (portrayed by [Jameela Jamil](/wiki/Jameela_Jamil "Jameela Jamil")), also known as **Titania**, is a [social media influencer](/wiki/Social_media_influencer "Social media influencer") with incredible strength who is obsessed with [She-Hulk](/wiki/Jennifer_Walters_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Jennifer Walters (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), ultimately becoming her rival.

(portrayed by [Lauren Ridloff](/wiki/Lauren_Ridloff "Lauren Ridloff")) is an [Eternal](/wiki/Eternals_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Eternals (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who can move at superhuman speeds. She is implied to harbour romantic feelings for [Druig](/wiki/Druig_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Druig (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

(portrayed by [Christopher Eccleston](/wiki/Christopher_Eccleston "Christopher Eccleston")) is the king of the [Dark Elves](/wiki/Dark_Elves_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Dark Elves (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who faces [Thor](/wiki/Thor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in 2013.[[3]](#cite_note-Eccleston-3) Eons ago, he faces Odin's father Bor and is defeated, being exiled to an alternate reality. He is awakened when the Aether is released, and he merges with it, plotting to submerge Earth in darkness during the convergence. He is killed when his own ship crushes him on Svartalfheim.

(motion-captured by [Jon Watts](/wiki/Jon_Watts "Jon Watts") and voiced by [Thomas Haden Church](/wiki/Thomas_Haden_Church "Thomas Haden Church")),[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) also known as the **Sandman**, is a small-time thief from an alternate universe who received an ability to transform into sand. He is one of the villains imprisoned by Doctor Strange, and rejects the cure that Spider-Man (Peter-1) attempts to give him. He is eventually cured and safely sent to his own dimension.

(portrayed by [Michael Rosenbaum](/wiki/Michael_Rosenbaum "Michael Rosenbaum")) is a member of Stakar Ogord's team of Ravagers. He visits [Yondu Udonta](#Yondu_Udonta) on Contraxia along with Stakar to remind him that he is in exile for breaking the Ravagers' code. After Yondu sacrifices himself to save [Peter Quill](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Martinex and Stakar are moved by his sacrifice, and he and the other Ravagers attend his funeral. The two of them decide to bring the rest of the team back together to honor him.

(portrayed by [Annette Bening](/wiki/Annette_Bening "Annette Bening")) is a [Kree](/wiki/Kree_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") scientist who rejected her species' war with the [Skrulls](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). She fled to Earth in the 1980s and adopted the alias of Dr. **Wendy Lawson**, a physicist at [Project Pegasus](/wiki/Project_Pegasus_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Project Pegasus (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Using the [Tesseract](/wiki/Tesseract_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), she sought to develop an experimental engine that would have allowed the Skrulls to settle beyond the reach of the Kree Empire. She is killed by [Yon-Rogg](/wiki/Yon-Rogg_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Yon-Rogg (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), though she is able to instruct [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to destroy the engine before Yon-Rogg is able to seize the device. The [Supreme Intelligence](/wiki/Supreme_Intelligence_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Supreme Intelligence (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") later takes on her appearance while conversing with Danvers.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

(voiced by [Sarah Natochenny](/wiki/Sarah_Natochenny "Sarah Natochenny"))[[8]](#cite_note-CastYFNSM-8) is a Russian criminal in an alternate universe who is part of a group of Russian bank robbers. During a robbery, Mila's accomplices—[Dmitri Smerdyakov](#Dmitri_Smerdyakov_/_Chameleon), [Mikhail Sytsevich](/wiki/Mikhail_Sytsevich "Mikhail Sytsevich"), and Roxanna Volkov—are arrested, while she escapes with the stolen money and takes it to the renegade scientist [Otto Octavius](#Otto_Octavius_/_Doctor_Octopus), who sells Mila a high-tech helmet that shoots lasers and analyzes and predicts opponent's movement patterns. Mila, calling herself **Unicorn**, uses the helmet to help her cohorts escape from prison and nearly kills [Spider-Man](/wiki/Spider-Man_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Spider-Man (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), but is stopped by Mikhail, who removes Mila's helmet, and Spider-Man apprehends them both.

(portrayed by [Michael Chernus](/wiki/Michael_Chernus "Michael Chernus"))[[9]](#cite_note-auto-9) is a weapons maker and part of a salvage company alongside [Adrian Toomes](#Adrian_Toomes_/_Vulture), [Herman Schultz](#Herman_Schultz_/_Shocker), and [Jackson Brice](/wiki/J

## M

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: M")]

### Mary MacPherran / Titania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mary MacPherran / Titania")]

See also: [Titania (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Titania_(Marvel_Comics) "Titania (Marvel Comics)")

**Mary MacPherran** (portrayed by [Jameela Jamil](/wiki/Jameela_Jamil "Jameela Jamil")), also known as **Titania**, is a [social media influencer](/wiki/Social_media_influencer "Social media influencer") with incredible strength who is obsessed with [She-Hulk](/wiki/Jennifer_Walters_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Jennifer Walters (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), ultimately becoming her rival.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+") series *[She-Hulk: Attorney at Law](/wiki/She-Hulk:_Attorney_at_Law "She-Hulk: Attorney at Law")* (2022).[[1]](#cite_note-1)

### Brett Mahoney

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Brett Mahoney")]

Main article: [Brett Mahoney (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Brett_Mahoney_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Brett Mahoney (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

### Makkari

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Makkari")]

See also: [Makkari (character)](/wiki/Makkari_(character) "Makkari (character)")

**Makkari** (portrayed by [Lauren Ridloff](/wiki/Lauren_Ridloff "Lauren Ridloff")) is an [Eternal](/wiki/Eternals_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Eternals (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who can move at superhuman speeds. She is implied to harbour romantic feelings for [Druig](/wiki/Druig_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Druig (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

The character is the first deaf superhero in the MCU. Ridloff stated that she started running and muscle-building to have "the symmetry of somebody who looks like a sprinter".[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Eternals](/wiki/Eternals_(film) "Eternals (film)")* (2021).[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2)

### Malekith the Accursed

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Malekith the Accursed")]

See also: [Malekith the Accursed](/wiki/Malekith_the_Accursed "Malekith the Accursed")

**Malekith the Accursed** (portrayed by [Christopher Eccleston](/wiki/Christopher_Eccleston "Christopher Eccleston")) is the king of the [Dark Elves](/wiki/Dark_Elves_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Dark Elves (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who faces [Thor](/wiki/Thor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in 2013.[[3]](#cite_note-Eccleston-3) Eons ago, he faces Odin's father Bor and is defeated, being exiled to an alternate reality. He is awakened when the Aether is released, and he merges with it, plotting to submerge Earth in darkness during the convergence. He is killed when his own ship crushes him on Svartalfheim.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Thor: The Dark World](/wiki/Thor:_The_Dark_World "Thor: The Dark World")* (2013). An alternate universe variant of Malekith (voiced by [Steven French](/wiki/Steven_French_(actor) "Steven French (actor)")) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *[What If...?](/wiki/What_If...%3F_(TV_series) "What If...? (TV series)")* (2021–2024).

### Flint Marko / Sandman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Flint Marko / Sandman")]

See also: [Sandman (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Sandman_(Marvel_Comics) "Sandman (Marvel Comics)")

**Flint Marko** (motion-captured by [Jon Watts](/wiki/Jon_Watts "Jon Watts") and voiced by [Thomas Haden Church](/wiki/Thomas_Haden_Church "Thomas Haden Church")),[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) also known as the **Sandman**, is a small-time thief from an alternate universe who received an ability to transform into sand. He is one of the villains imprisoned by Doctor Strange, and rejects the cure that Spider-Man (Peter-1) attempts to give him. He is eventually cured and safely sent to his own dimension.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Spider-Man: No Way Home](/wiki/Spider-Man:_No_Way_Home "Spider-Man: No Way Home")* (2021). Church reprises his role from [Sam Raimi](/wiki/Sam_Raimi "Sam Raimi")'s film *[Spider-Man 3](/wiki/Spider-Man_3 "Spider-Man 3")* (2007).

### Martinex

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Martinex")]

See also: [Martinex](/wiki/Martinex "Martinex")

**Martinex** (portrayed by [Michael Rosenbaum](/wiki/Michael_Rosenbaum "Michael Rosenbaum")) is a member of Stakar Ogord's team of Ravagers. He visits [Yondu Udonta](#Yondu_Udonta) on Contraxia along with Stakar to remind him that he is in exile for breaking the Ravagers' code. After Yondu sacrifices himself to save [Peter Quill](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Martinex and Stakar are moved by his sacrifice, and he and the other Ravagers attend his funeral. The two of them decide to bring the rest of the team back together to honor him.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._2 "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2")* (2017) and *[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._3 "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3")* (2023).[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Mar-Vell / Wendy Lawson

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Mar-Vell / Wendy Lawson")]

See also: [Captain Marvel (Mar-Vell)](/wiki/Captain_Marvel_(Mar-Vell) "Captain Marvel (Mar-Vell)")

**Mar-Vell** (portrayed by [Annette Bening](/wiki/Annette_Bening "Annette Bening")) is a [Kree](/wiki/Kree_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") scientist who rejected her species' war with the [Skrulls](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). She fled to Earth in the 1980s and adopted the alias of Dr. **Wendy Lawson**, a physicist at [Project Pegasus](/wiki/Project_Pegasus_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Project Pegasus (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Using the [Tesseract](/wiki/Tesseract_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), she sought to develop an experimental engine that would have allowed the Skrulls to settle beyond the reach of the Kree Empire. She is killed by [Yon-Rogg](/wiki/Yon-Rogg_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Yon-Rogg (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), though she is able to instruct [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to destroy the engine before Yon-Rogg is able to seize the device. The [Supreme Intelligence](/wiki/Supreme_Intelligence_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Supreme Intelligence (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") later takes on her appearance while conversing with Danvers.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Captain Marvel](/wiki/Captain_Marvel_(film) "Captain Marvel (film)")* (2019). An alternate universe variant of Mar-Vell (voiced by Keri Tombazian) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Mila Masaryk / Unicorn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Mila Masaryk / Unicorn")]

See also: [Unicorn (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Unicorn_(Marvel_Comics) "Unicorn (Marvel Comics)")

**Mila Masaryk** (voiced by [Sarah Natochenny](/wiki/Sarah_Natochenny "Sarah Natochenny"))[[8]](#cite_note-CastYFNSM-8) is a Russian criminal in an alternate universe who is part of a group of Russian bank robbers. During a robbery, Mila's accomplices—[Dmitri Smerdyakov](#Dmitri_Smerdyakov_/_Chameleon), [Mikhail Sytsevich](/wiki/Mikhail_Sytsevich "Mikhail Sytsevich"), and Roxanna Volkov—are arrested, while she escapes with the stolen money and takes it to the renegade scientist [Otto Octavius](#Otto_Octavius_/_Doctor_Octopus), who sells Mila a high-tech helmet that shoots lasers and analyzes and predicts opponent's movement patterns. Mila, calling herself **Unicorn**, uses the helmet to help her cohorts escape from prison and nearly kills [Spider-Man](/wiki/Spider-Man_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Spider-Man (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), but is stopped by Mikhail, who removes Mila's helmet, and Spider-Man apprehends them both.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *[Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man](/wiki/Your_Friendly_Neighborhood_Spider-Man "Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man")* (2025–present).

### Phineas Mason

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Phineas Mason")]

See also: [Tinkerer (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Tinkerer_(Marvel_Comics) "Tinkerer (Marvel Comics)")

**Phineas Mason** (portrayed by [Michael Chernus](/wiki/Michael_Chernus "Michael Chernus"))[[9]](#cite_note-auto-9) is a weapons maker and part of a salvage company alongside [Adrian Toomes](#Adrian_Toomes_/_Vulture), [Herman Schultz](#Herman_Schultz_/_Shocker), and [Jackson Brice](/wiki/Jackson_Brice_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Jackson Brice (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). When the salvaging company goes out of business due to the [Department of Damage Control](/wiki/Department_of_Damage_Control "Department of Damage Control"), Mason helps Toomes steal leftover technology from the [Avengers](/wiki/Avengers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")' battles and build advanced weapons out of the technology, such as Toomes's flight suit and modified versions of [Brock Rumlow](#Brock_Rumlow)'s vibro-blast emitting gauntlets. While his assistants were defeated by [Spider-Man](/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and arrested by the authorities, Mason's fate is left unknown.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Spider-Man: Homecoming](/wiki/Spider-Man:_Homecoming "Spider-Man: Homecoming")* (2017).[[9]](#cite_note-auto-9)

### Rick Mason

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Rick Mason")]

See also: [Agent (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Agent_(Marvel_Comics) "Agent (Marvel Comics)")

**Rick Mason** (portrayed by [O-T Fagbenle](/wiki/O-T_Fagbenle "O-T Fagbenle")) is an old friend of [Natasha Romanoff](/wiki/Natasha_Romanoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s [S.H.I.E.L.D.](/wiki/S.H.I.E.L.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") past. In 2016, he helps her while she is a fugitive. In 2026, he assists [Nick Fury](/wiki/Nick_Fury_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the film *[Black Widow](/wiki/Black_Widow_(2021_film) "Black Widow (2021 film)")* (2021); and the Disney+ series *[Secret Invasion](/wiki/Secret_Invasion_(miniseries) "Secret Invasion (miniseries)")* (2023).

### Ebony Maw

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Ebony Maw")]

See also: [Ebony Maw](/wiki/Ebony_Maw "Ebony Maw")

**Ebony Maw** (voiced and portrayed via [motion capture](/wiki/Motion_capture "Motion capture") by [Tom Vaughan-Lawlor](/wiki/Tom_Vaughan-Lawlor "Tom Vaughan-Lawlor")) is an adopted son of [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and member of the [Black Order](/wiki/Black_Order_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Black Order (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who is killed in space after being defeated by Tony Stark and Peter Parker. A past version of Maw from 2014 travels through time with Thanos's forces to stop the Avengers from foiling his plans. However, they are all disintegrated when Stark uses the [Infinity Gauntlet](/wiki/Infinity_Gauntlet_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Avengers: Infinity War](/wiki/Avengers:_Infinity_War "Avengers: Infinity War")* (2018). Alternate universe variants of Maw appeared in two projects: the film *[Avengers: Endgame](/wiki/Avengers:_Endgame "Avengers: Endgame")* (2019); and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10): 2

### Billy Maximoff / William Kaplan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Billy Maximoff / William Kaplan")]

See also: [Wiccan (character)](/wiki/Wiccan_(character) "Wiccan (character)")

**William** "**Billy**" **Maximoff** (portrayed by Baylen Bielitz as a five-year old and [Julian Hilliard](/wiki/Julian_Hilliard "Julian Hilliard") as a ten-year old)[[11]](#cite_note-BillyTommy-11) is the son of [Wanda Maximoff](/wiki/Wanda_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Vision](/wiki/Vision_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and the younger twin brother of [Tommy Maximoff](#Tommy_Maximoff). He was created by Wanda using [chaos magic](/wiki/Chaos_magic_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Chaos magic (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") within the [Westview anomaly](/wiki/Westview_anomaly "Westview anomaly") in [Westview, New Jersey](/wiki/Westview,_New_Jersey "Westview, New Jersey"), a reality modeled after a [fictional sitcom](/wiki/WandaVision_sitcom "WandaVision sitcom") inspired by those Wanda watched as a child whose residents are [mind-controlled](/wiki/Brainwashing "Brainwashing") by Wanda. After Wanda "gives birth" to Billy, he ages rapidly to five and ten years old, acquiring supernatural abilities: Billy harnesses chaos magic like his mother. Following a battle between Wanda and the witch [Agatha Harkness](/wiki/Agatha_Harkness_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Wanda dissolves the Westview anomaly, erasing the imaginary existence of Billy. Billy reincarnates upon his "death" in the body of **William Kaplan** (portrayed by [Joe Locke](/wiki/Joe_Locke "Joe Locke")), a 13-year-old from Westview's neighboring town of Eastview, who was killed in a car accident after leaving his [bar mitzvah](/wiki/Bar_mitzvah "Bar mitzvah") early; stricken with amnesia, Billy then assumes William's identity, unaware of his past life.

Following Wanda's death, Billy's curiosity about the anomaly that occurred the day of his car crash leads him to investigate and learn that Wanda and Vision had children in Westview, one of whom had the same abilities that he has. Realizing his true identity, he becomes determined to find his lost brother. He frees Harkness from a spell placed by Wanda and asks that she take him on the [Witches' Road](/wiki/Witches%27_Road "Witches' Road"), a magical pathway where one's desires are granted after completing a series of "trials". In the final trial, Billy locates Tommy's soul and places it in the body of a drowning boy. After discovering that the Witches' Road was a myth created by Harkness and that he had willed it into existence, he accepts the mentorship of Harkness, who dies and returns as a ghost, and set out to search for Tommy.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the Disney+ series *[WandaVision](/wiki/WandaVision "WandaVision")* (2021) and *[Agatha All Along](/wiki/Agatha_All_Along "Agatha All Along")* (2024). An alternate universe variant of Billy appeared in one project: the film *[Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness](/wiki/Doctor_Strange_in_the_Multiverse_of_Madness "Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness")* (2022).

#### Kaplan family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Kaplan family")]

William Kaplan's family plays an important role in his life. **Jeff** (portrayed by [Paul Adelstein](/wiki/Paul_Adelstein "Paul Adelstein")) and **Rebecca Kaplan** (portrayed by [Maria Dizzia](/wiki/Maria_Dizzia "Maria Dizzia")) are William's parents.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the characters have appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Agatha All Along*.

### Pietro Maximoff

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Pietro Maximoff")]

See also: [Quicksilver (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Quicksilver_(Marvel_Comics) "Quicksilver (Marvel Comics)")

**Pietro Maximoff** (portrayed by [Aaron Taylor-Johnson](/wiki/Aaron_Taylor-Johnson "Aaron Taylor-Johnson") as an adult and Gabriel Gurevich as a child) is Wanda Maximoff's twin brother. They are introduced as Hydra assets, both acquiring superhuman powers after volunteering to be experimented upon by the [Mind Stone](/wiki/Mind_Stone "Mind Stone"),[[13]](#cite_note-13) with Pietro gaining [superhuman speed](/wiki/Superhuman_speed "Superhuman speed"). Harboring a lifelong hatred for American arms manufacturer [Tony Stark](/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), whose bombs killed their parents, they side with [Ultron](/wiki/Ultron_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") against the Avengers before later switching sides. In the final conflict with Ultron, Pietro dies a hero's death, saving the lives of [Clint Barton](/wiki/Clint_Barton_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and a [Sokovian](/wiki/Sokovia "Sokovia") boy. In 2023, the witch [Agatha Harkness](/wiki/Agatha_Harkness_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") forces Westview resident **[Ralph Bohner](/wiki/Ralph_Bohner "Ralph Bohner")** (portrayed by [Evan Peters](/wiki/Evan_Peters "Evan Peters")) to impersonate Pietro to get close to Wanda within her created alternate reality.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The character's reception has been lukewarm with complaints that the character or his power was not developed enough compared to the [comic book version](/wiki/Quicksilver_(Marvel_Comics) "Quicksilver (Marvel Comics)") or [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox")'s [*X-Men* film series](/wiki/X-Men_(film_series) "X-Men (film series)") [version](/wiki/Peter_Maximoff "Peter Maximoff").[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *[Captain America: The Winter Soldier](/wiki/Captain_America:_The_Winter_Soldier "Captain America: The Winter Soldier")* (2014) ([mid-credits](/wiki/Mid-credits_scene "Mid-credits scene") cameo) and *[Avengers: Age of Ultron](/wiki/Avengers:_Age_of_Ultron "Avengers: Age of Ultron")* (2015);[[18]](#cite_note-Sneider-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20) and the Disney+ series *WandaVision*.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### Tommy Maximoff

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Tommy Maximoff")]

See also: [Speed (character)](/wiki/Speed_(character) "Speed (character)")

**Thomas** "**Tommy**" **Maximoff** (portrayed by Gavin Borders as a five-year old and [Jett Klyne](/wiki/Jett_Klyne "Jett Klyne") as a ten-year old)[[11]](#cite_note-BillyTommy-11) is the son of [Wanda Maximoff](/wiki/Wanda_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Vision](/wiki/Vision_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and the older twin brother of [Billy Maximoff](#Billy_Maximoff_/_William_Kaplan). He was created by Wanda using [chaos magic](/wiki/Chaos_magic_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Chaos magic (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") within the [Westview anomaly](/wiki/Westview_anomaly "Westview anomaly") in [Westview, New Jersey](/wiki/Westview,_New_Jersey "Westview, New Jersey"), a reality modeled after a [fictional sitcom](/wiki/WandaVision_sitcom "WandaVision sitcom") inspired by those Wanda watched as a child whose residents are [mind-controlled](/wiki/Brainwashing "Brainwashing") by Wanda. After Wanda "gives birth" to Tommy, he ages rapidly to five and ten years old, acquiring supernatural abilities: Tommy gains [superhuman speed](/wiki/Superhuman_speed "Superhuman speed") like his uncle "[Pietro Maximoff](/wiki/Pietro_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Pietro Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")" (in reality, [Ralph Bohner](/wiki/Ralph_Bohner "Ralph Bohner") in disguise). Following a battle between Wanda and the witch [Agatha Harkness](/wiki/Agatha_Harkness_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Wanda dissolves the Westview anomaly, erasing the imaginary existence of Tommy. While traveling the [Witches' Road](/wiki/Witches%27_Road "Witches' Road"), Agatha helps a reincarnated Billy connect with Tommy's soul and place it in the body of a teenager who had drowned during a botched prank.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *WandaVision*. He will return as **Thomas Shepherd** (portrayed by [Ruaridh Mollica](/wiki/Ruaridh_Mollica "Ruaridh Mollica")) in the upcoming Disney+ series *[VisionQuest](/wiki/VisionQuest "VisionQuest")* (2026).[[22]](#cite_note-NYCC2025EW-22) An alternate universe variant of Tommy appeared in one project: the film *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*.

### Wanda Maximoff / Scarlet Witch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Wanda Maximoff / Scarlet Witch")]

Main article: [Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Wanda_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

### M'Baku

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: M'Baku")]

See also: [Man-Ape](/wiki/Man-Ape "Man-Ape")

**M'Baku** (portrayed by [Winston Duke](/wiki/Winston_Duke "Winston Duke")) is the leader of the renegade [Jabari](/wiki/Jabari_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Jabari (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") Tribe, who shun [Wakanda](/wiki/Wakanda_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s technological society and have a religious reverence for gorillas, such as decorating their armor with white gorilla pelts and worshiping the ape god, [Hanuman](/wiki/Hanuman "Hanuman"), as a part of a complex [Indo-African](/wiki/Non-resident_Indian_and_person_of_Indian_origin "Non-resident Indian and person of Indian origin") religion rather than the Panther god [Bast](/wiki/Bast_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bast (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24) In *Black Panther*, M'Baku challenges [T'Challa](/wiki/T%27Challa_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") for control of Wakanda. When he is defeated in combat, the latter convinces him to yield. M'Baku returns the favor by looking after the wounded T'Challa following the latter's fight with [N'Jadaka](#N'Jadaka_/_Erik_"Killmonger"_Stevens) and agreeing to look after [Ramonda](#Ramonda). He initially declines T'Challa's request to help fight N'Jadaka, but ultimately reconsiders and leads the Jabari into battle against him. Following N'Jadaka's death, M'Baku is granted a place on Wakanda's national council.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Later, in *Avengers: Infinity War*, he and the Jabari join the battle against Thanos's [Outriders](/wiki/Outriders_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Outriders (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"); M'Baku survives [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip").[[26]](#cite_note-26) In 2023, he joins the final battle against a past version of Thanos. In 2025, following T'Challa's and Ramonda's deaths, and respecting M'Baku's advice during the Talokan—Wakanda War, Shuri abdicates the Wakandan throne and allows M'Baku to challenge for it in her absence, leading him to become the new King of Wakanda.[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *[Black Panther](/wiki/Black_Panther_(film) "Black Panther (film)")* (2018), *Avengers: Infinity War*, *Avengers: Endgame*, and *[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever](/wiki/Black_Panther:_Wakanda_Forever "Black Panther: Wakanda Forever")* (2022). He will return in the film *[Avengers: Doomsday](/wiki/Avengers:_Doomsday "Avengers: Doomsday")* (2026).[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29)

### Hank McCoy / Beast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Hank McCoy / Beast")]

See also: [Beast (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Beast_(Marvel_Comics) "Beast (Marvel Comics)")

Dr. **Henry** "**Hank**" **McCoy** (portrayed by [Kelsey Grammer](/wiki/Kelsey_Grammer "Kelsey Grammer")), also known as **Beast**, is a [mutant](/wiki/Mutant_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Mutant (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and a member of the [X-Men](/wiki/X-Men_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "X-Men (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") from an alternate universe who possesses animalistic physical characteristics that include blue fur, pointy ears, fangs, and claws.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[The Marvels](/wiki/The_Marvels "The Marvels")* (2023).[[30]](#cite_note-TheMarvelsPostCreditsScene-30) He will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29) Grammer previously portrayed a different version of McCoy in the 20th Century Fox films *[X-Men: The Last Stand](/wiki/X-Men:_The_Last_Stand "X-Men: The Last Stand")* (2006) and *[X-Men: Days of Future Past](/wiki/X-Men:_Days_of_Future_Past "X-Men: Days of Future Past")* (2014).[[30]](#cite_note-TheMarvelsPostCreditsScene-30)

### Kirsten McDuffie

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Kirsten McDuffie")]

See also: [Kirsten McDuffie](/wiki/Kirsten_McDuffie "Kirsten McDuffie")

**Kirsten McDuffie** (portrayed by [Nikki M. James](/wiki/Nikki_M._James "Nikki M. James"))[[31]](#cite_note-31): 5 is a former New York [assistant district attorney](/wiki/Assistant_district_attorney "Assistant district attorney") who became [Matt Murdock](/wiki/Matt_Murdock_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Matt Murdock (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s partner and founded the law firm [Murdock & McDuffie](/wiki/Murdock_%26_McDuffie "Murdock & McDuffie") with him.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *[Daredevil: Born Again](/wiki/Daredevil:_Born_Again "Daredevil: Born Again")* (2025–present).

### Mephisto

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Mephisto")]

See also: [Mephisto (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Mephisto_(Marvel_Comics) "Mephisto (Marvel Comics)")

**Mephisto** (portrayed by [Sacha Baron Cohen](/wiki/Sacha_Baron_Cohen "Sacha Baron Cohen"))[[32]](#cite_note-32) is a powerful extra-dimensional demon who manipulates humans into forming pacts with him.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *[Ironheart](/wiki/Ironheart_(miniseries) "Ironheart (miniseries)")* (2025).

### Proxima Midnight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Proxima Midnight")]

See also: [Proxima Midnight](/wiki/Proxima_Midnight "Proxima Midnight")

**Proxima Midnight** (voiced and faced by [Carrie Coon](/wiki/Carrie_Coon "Carrie Coon"), motion-captured by [Monique Ganderton](/wiki/Monique_Ganderton "Monique Ganderton")) is Thanos' adopted daughter. She joined her father in his quest for the six [Infinity Stones](/wiki/Infinity_Stones "Infinity Stones"), initially attacked the *Statesman* with her brothers, helping to kill the [Asgardians](/wiki/Asgard_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") aboard to retrieve the [Space Stone](/wiki/Space_Stone "Space Stone"). While attempting to claim the Mind Stone, she and [Corvus Glaive](/wiki/Corvus_Glaive_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Corvus Glaive (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") attack Wanda Maximoff and [Vision](/wiki/Vision_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), but are repelled by [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), [Sam Wilson](/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and Natasha Romanoff. During a second attempt at the Mind Stone, Proxima fights Romanoff and [Okoye](#Okoye), but is ultimately killed by Maximoff. A past version of Midnight from 2014 travels through time with Thanos's forces to stop the Avengers from foiling his plans. However, they are all disintegrated when Tony Stark uses the Infinity Gauntlet.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Avengers: Infinity War*. Alternate universe variants of Midnight appeared in two projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10): 2

### Miek

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Miek")]

See also: [Miek](/wiki/Miek "Miek")

**Miek** (motion-captured by Carly Rees and voiced by Stephen Murdoch) is a [Sakaaran](/wiki/Sakaarans_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sakaarans (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") insectoid warrior. This version of the character is a larva-like creature as opposed to a humanoid roach [like in the comic books](/wiki/Miek "Miek"), and initially uses an exoskeleton equipped with blades in combat. Having been freed from the [Grandmaster](/wiki/Grandmaster_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Grandmaster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s prison, Miek fights alongside [Thor](/wiki/Thor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Korg](/wiki/Korg_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Korg (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and joins the Asgardian people in their journey to Earth after the destruction of Asgard. Along with Korg, he survives Thanos's attack on the Asgardian starship and the Blip. He finds a new home with the Asgardians in New Asgard in Norway. Miek participates in the final battle at the destroyed Avengers Headquarters against Thanos and his army. Sometime later, Miek metamorphoses to a female form, and she organises tourism in New Asgard.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *[Thor: Ragnarok](/wiki/Thor:_Ragnarok "Thor: Ragnarok")* (2017), *Avengers: Endgame*, and *[Thor: Love and Thunder](/wiki/Thor:_Love_and_Thunder "Thor: Love and Thunder")* (2022). Alternate universe variants of Miek appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Minn-Erva

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Minn-Erva")]

See also: [Doctor Minerva](/wiki/Doctor_Minerva "Doctor Minerva")

**Minn-Erva** (portrayed by [Gemma Chan](/wiki/Gemma_Chan "Gemma Chan")) is a [Kree](/wiki/Kree_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") sniper and member of [Starforce](/wiki/Starforce_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Starforce (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who bears a strong animosity towards teammate [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). After Danvers ends up on Earth, Minn-Erva accompanies Starforce to the planet to rescue her, where they discover a group of [Skrull](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") refugees and attempt to kill them. Having discovered the truth of the Kree's war with the Skrulls, Danvers fights off the rest of Starforce while Minn-Erva is shot down by [Maria Rambeau](#Maria_Rambeau).

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Captain Marvel*.

### Nico Minoru

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Nico Minoru")]

See also: [Nico Minoru](/wiki/Nico_Minoru "Nico Minoru")

**Nico Minoru** (portrayed by [Lyrica Okano](/wiki/Lyrica_Okano "Lyrica Okano")) is a [Wiccan](/wiki/Wicca "Wicca") and a member of the [Runaways](/wiki/Runaways_(comics) "Runaways (comics)") who isolates herself with her [gothic](/wiki/Gothic_fashion "Gothic fashion") appearance.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the [Marvel Television](/wiki/Marvel_Television "Marvel Television") series *[Runaways](/wiki/Runaways_(TV_series) "Runaways (TV series)")* (2017–2019). An alternate universe variant of Nico appeared in two projects: the comic book series *[Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man](/wiki/Your_Friendly_Neighborhood_Spider-Man_(comics) "Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man (comics)")* (2024–2025);[[33]](#cite_note-PrequelComicYFNSM-33) and the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man* (voiced by Grace Song).[[34]](#cite_note-101CopyrightCastYFNSM-34)

### Tina Minoru

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Tina Minoru")]

See also: [Tina Minoru](/wiki/Tina_Minoru "Tina Minoru")

**Tina Minoru** (initially portrayed by [Linda Louise Duan](/wiki/Linda_Louise_Duan "Linda Louise Duan") and later by [Brittany Ishibashi](/wiki/Brittany_Ishibashi "Brittany Ishibashi"))[[35]](#cite_note-35)[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-PrideCastAnnounce-37) is the mother of [Nico Minoru](#Nico_Minoru) and a member of the [Pride](/wiki/Pride_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Pride (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the film *[Doctor Strange](/wiki/Doctor_Strange_(2016_film) "Doctor Strange (2016 film)")* (2016); and the Marvel Television series *Runaways*.

### Mobius M. Mobius

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Mobius M. Mobius")]

See also: [Mobius M. Mobius](/wiki/Mobius_M._Mobius "Mobius M. Mobius")

**Mobius M. Mobius** (portrayed by [Owen Wilson](/wiki/Owen_Wilson "Owen Wilson")) is an agent of the [Time Variance Authority](/wiki/Time_Variance_Authority_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") specialized in the investigation of particularly dangerous "time variant" criminals. He befriends a [2012 variant of Loki](/wiki/Loki_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#2012_variant "Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). He travels through time and realities with Loki to find the variant Sylvie. While helping Loki to handle the time slipping, it's revealed that Mobius has been with the TVA for at least four hundred years, although he has undergone multiple memory wipes. In "[Science/Fiction](/wiki/Science/Fiction "Science/Fiction")", Mobius is revealed to be a temporal variant of **Don**, a jet ski salesman from 2022 [Cleveland](/wiki/Cleveland "Cleveland") who is also the single father of two sons, Kevin and Sean. In the season finale, Mobius enters Earth-616, the main universe, and observes his main counterpart at his house.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the Disney+ series *[Loki](/wiki/Loki_(TV_series) "Loki (TV series)")* (2021–2023);[[38]](#cite_note-38) the film *[Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania](/wiki/Ant-Man_and_the_Wasp:_Quantumania "Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania")* (2023) (post-credits cameo);[[39]](#cite_note-39) and the comic book series *[TVA](/wiki/TVA_(comic_book) "TVA (comic book)")* (2024–2025).[[40]](#cite_note-BlairTVAMarvelPodcast-40)

### Anton Mogart

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Anton Mogart")]

See also: [Midnight Man (comics)](/wiki/Midnight_Man_(comics) "Midnight Man (comics)")

**Anton Mogart** (portrayed by [Gaspard Ulliel](/wiki/Gaspard_Ulliel "Gaspard Ulliel")) is a wealthy antiquities collector living in Egypt and an old acquaintance of [Layla El-Faouly](/wiki/Layla_El-Faouly "Layla El-Faouly"). [Marc Spector, Steven Grant](/wiki/Marc_Spector_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Marc Spector (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and El-Faouly meet with him to find the map to [Ammit](/wiki/Ammit_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ammit (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s tomb. He betrays them when [Arthur Harrow](/wiki/Arthur_Harrow "Arthur Harrow") arrives and is speared through the chest by Spector.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *[Moon Knight](/wiki/Moon_Knight_(miniseries) "Moon Knight (miniseries)")* (2022).[[41]](#cite_note-41)

### Karl Mordo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Karl Mordo")]

See also: [Baron Mordo](/wiki/Baron_Mordo "Baron Mordo")

**Karl Mordo** (portrayed by [Chiwetel Ejiofor](/wiki/Chiwetel_Ejiofor "Chiwetel Ejiofor")) is a sorcerer and a former member of the [Masters of the Mystic Arts](/wiki/Masters_of_the_Mystic_Arts "Masters of the Mystic Arts"). A close ally to the [Ancient One](/wiki/Ancient_One_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), he assists in recruiting and training their future sorcerers. In this role, he trains [Stephen Strange](/wiki/Stephen_Strange_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), using the Staff of the Living Tribunal as his weapon. He also helps Strange fight against [Kaecilius](/wiki/Kaecilius_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). The Ancient One notes that Mordo's strength must be balanced by Strange since Mordo is unable to recognize the need for moral flexibility and compromise. In 2017, disillusioned with the teachings of the Ancient One after learning that the latter's immortality has been the result of her drawing on the energy of the [Dark Dimension](/wiki/Dark_Dimension_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Dark Dimension (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and Strange further breaking nature's laws, he decides to leave his fellow sorcerers. He comes to believe that all magic perverts and disrupts the natural order, thus causing him to set out to stop others from using it. Mordo later visits former Master [Jonathan Pangborn](/wiki/Jonathan_Pangborn "Jonathan Pangborn") and steals the energy that he uses to walk, stating that the world has "too many sorcerers".[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In an alternate universe designated as [Earth-838](/wiki/Earth-838 "Earth-838"), [Baron](/wiki/Baron "Baron") Karl Mordo is the Sorcerer Supreme and a member of the [Illuminati](/wiki/Illuminati_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Illuminati (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), following the betrayal of his universe's Stephen Strange.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Doctor Strange*. An alternate universe variant of Mordo appeared in one project: the film *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*.

### Karli Morgenthau

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Karli Morgenthau")]

See also: [Karl Morgenthau](/wiki/Karl_Morgenthau "Karl Morgenthau")

**Karli Morgenthau** (portrayed by [Erin Kellyman](/wiki/Erin_Kellyman "Erin Kellyman")) is the teenage leader of the anti-nationalist terrorist group the [Flag Smashers](/wiki/Flag_Smashers "Flag Smashers"), who were made Super Soldiers with the help of the Power Broker ([Sharon Carter](/wiki/Sharon_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sharon Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")) and use violent tactics such as bombings to achieve [open borders](/wiki/Open_border "Open border") for refugees in the [Baltic states](/wiki/Baltic_states "Baltic states"). She is killed by Carter (to hide Carter's identity as the Power Broker) after she attempts to kill Carter and an oblivious Sam Wilson.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *[The Falcon and the Winter Soldier](/wiki/The_Falcon_and_the_Winter_Soldier "The Falcon and the Winter Soldier")* (2021).[[42]](#cite_note-42)

**James** "**Jim**" **Morita** (portrayed by [Kenneth Choi](/wiki/Kenneth_Choi "Kenneth Choi")) is a [Japanese American](/wiki/Japanese_American "Japanese American") member of the [Howling Commandos](/wiki/Howling_Commandos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Howling Commandos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who fought in [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").

"Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.")* (2013–2020).[[43]](#cite_note-43) An alternate universe variant of Morita appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

## N

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: N")]

### Najma

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Najma")]

**Najma** (portrayed by [Nimra Bucha](/wiki/Nimra_Bucha "Nimra Bucha")) is Kamran's mother, and the leader of the [Clandestines](/wiki/Clandestines_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Clandestines (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), who want to return to their home dimension at any cost necessary. In 1942, Najma discovered one of the bangles at a Ten Rings temple in India, and gave it to Aisha for safekeeping when was then attacked by the British Armed Forces. She found Aisha in an Indian village five years later, during the Partition of India, and asked Aisha to return the bangle and come with her so they could return home. When she realized Aisha had no intention of relinquishing the bangle or going back home with them, Najma stabbed Aisha and left her to die. In 2025, Najma sensed that Kamala Khan had Aisha's bangle and realized Aisha had started a family. In a fight with Khan, she ended up hitting the bangle and unintentionally opened a rift to the Noor Dimension. Najma was convinced by Khan not to abandon Kamran, and Najma chose to sacrifice herself to close the rift, although not before passing on some of the Noor from the rift on to her son.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *[Ms. Marvel](/wiki/Ms._Marvel_(miniseries) "Ms. Marvel (miniseries)")* (2022).

### Nakia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Nakia")]

See also: [Nakia (comics)](/wiki/Nakia_(comics) "Nakia (comics)")

**Nakia** (portrayed by [Lupita Nyong'o](/wiki/Lupita_Nyong%27o "Lupita Nyong'o"))[[46]](#cite_note-46) is a former [Dora Milaje](/wiki/Dora_Milaje_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Dora Milaje (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), a member of the War Dogs, and T'Challa's lover. Having often been sent on missions around the world and witnessing the hardships experienced by many people, she grows in the belief that Wakanda should actively help them. Nakia returns to Wakanda upon hearing that T'Challa's father, [T'Chaka](#T'Chaka_/_Black_Panther), was killed. She stays after T'Challa is crowned king and asks that she accompany him to one of their missions. After N'Jadaka seizes the throne and orders the heart-shaped herbs which grant the power of the Black Panther to be burned, Nakia steals one. Although Ramonda urges Nakia to consume it to challenge N'Jadaka, Nakia plans to offer it to M'Baku so that he can challenge N'Jadaka with his army. M'Baku reveals that his people have recovered T'Challa, so Nakia gives the herb to T'Challa, healing him and restoring his powers as the Black Panther. Nakia then assists in the insurrection against N'Jadaka, dressing as a Dora Milaje soldier at [Shuri](/wiki/Shuri_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s insistence. Following N'Jadaka's death, Nakia resumes her relationship with T'Challa and accepts a position running a Wakandan outreach center in California at the location of [N'Jobu](/wiki/N%27Jobu "N'Jobu") and N'Jadaka's former apartment. Sometime after, Nakia and T'Challa have a child, also named T'Challa, and decide to have him be raised away from the throne. After Thanos's attack on Wakanda and the ensuing [Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip"), Nakia leaves Wakanda and raises her child in [Haiti](/wiki/Haiti "Haiti").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Black Panther* and *Black Panther: Wakanda Forever*. An alternate universe variant of Nakia (voiced by [Brittany Adebumola](/wiki/Brittany_Adebumola "Brittany Adebumola")) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Namor / K'uk'ulkan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Namor / K'uk'ulkan")]

See also: [Namor](/wiki/Namor "Namor")

**Namor** (portrayed by [Tenoch Huerta Mejía](/wiki/Tenoch_Huerta_Mej%C3%ADa "Tenoch Huerta Mejía") as an adult and by Manuel Chavez as a child)[[47]](#cite_note-47)[[48]](#cite_note-48)[[49]](#cite_note-49) is the king of [Talokan](/wiki/Talokan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Talokan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"),[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51) whose people refer to him as the feathered serpent god **[K'uk'ulkan](/wiki/Kukulkan "Kukulkan")**.[[52]](#cite_note-52) In 1571, his mother Fen ingested a vibranium-laced herb to gain immunity from smallpox while pregnant with her son. The effects of the herb caused her and the rest of [Yucatán](/wiki/Yucat%C3%A1n "Yucatán")'s people to develop blue skin and grow gills that restricted their ability to breathe air on the surface, thus forcing them to relocate underwater and establish Talokan as a new civilization. The herb additionally caused her son to undergo a [mutation](/wiki/Mutant_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Mutant (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") at birth, which gave him pointed ears, winged ankles enabling him to fly, and the ability to breathe oxygen and water simultaneously. Motivated by his hatred of the surface world, and a recent discovery of a vibranium detection device designed by Riri Williams in the ocean that put his nation at risk, he initially attempted to forge a military alliance with the nation of [Wakanda](/wiki/Wakanda_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to protect Talokan while supplying their people with his army due to its similarly isolationist nature in exchange for the custody of Williams, to which both Queen Ramonda and Princess Shuri decline. However, after a fight between the two nations, Shuri and Namor come to an alliance, and opt to help each other in the future.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Black Panther: Wakanda Forever*.[[53]](#cite_note-53) He will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29) An alternate universe variant of Namor appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *[Marvel Zombies](/wiki/Marvel_Zombies_(miniseries) "Marvel Zombies (miniseries)")* (2025).[[54]](#cite_note-MarvelZombiesTrailer-54)

### N'Jadaka / Erik "Killmonger" Stevens

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: N'Jadaka / Erik \"Killmonger\" Stevens")]

See also: [Erik Killmonger](/wiki/Erik_Killmonger "Erik Killmonger")

**N'Jadaka** (portrayed by [Michael B. Jordan](/wiki/Michael_B._Jordan "Michael B. Jordan")), also known as **Erik Stevens** or **Killmonger**, is a former [U.S. Navy SEAL](/wiki/U.S._Navy_SEAL "U.S. Navy SEAL") [lieutenant](/wiki/Lieutenant_(navy) "Lieutenant (navy)") and the son of N'Jobu. His father was killed by T'Chaka for helping [Ulysses Klaue](/wiki/Ulysses_Klaue_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ulysses Klaue (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") smuggle [vibranium](/wiki/Vibranium_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") out of Wakanda to arm oppressed peoples around the world and initiate revolutions. N'Jadaka later becomes an acquaintance of Klaue, before betraying and killing him to gain the trust of the Wakandan population. After bringing the dead Klaue, he challenges his cousin T'Challa to a duel over the throne, which N'Jadaka wins. However, T'Challa later returns to take back his throne, with the help of M'Baku, Okoye, and Shuri. T'Challa defeats N'Jadaka in combat and fatally wounds him, although he takes N'Jadaka to see the Wakandan sunset per his wishes. N'Jadaka dies after refusing to be healed, claiming that death is "better than bondage". Impacted by his cousin's death, T'Challa decides to finally open Wakanda up to the rest of the world. Following the deaths of T'Challa and his and Shuri's mother Ramonda, N'Jadaka's spirit meets with Shuri in the ancestral plane after she ingests a synthetic version of the heart-shaped herb, warning her of how close her desire for vengeance upon [Namor](#Namor) is bringing her to being like him, with Shuri then assuming a Black Panther suit inspired by N'Jadaka's.

The character has been widely praised as one of the MCU's best villains,[[61]](#cite_note-61)[[62]](#cite_note-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63) with Ben Child of *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* comparing the character to both the [Terminator](/wiki/Terminator_(character) "Terminator (character)") and [Darth Vader](/wiki/Darth_Vader "Darth Vader").[[64]](#cite_note-64)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Black Panther* and *Black Panther: Wakanda Forever*; and the Disney+ animated series *[Eyes of Wakanda](/wiki/Eyes_of_Wakanda "Eyes of Wakanda")* (2025) (non-speaking cameo).[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66) An alternate universe variant of Killmonger appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[67]](#cite_note-WhatIfSDCC-67)[[68]](#cite_note-MarvelMustHavesFunkoLego-68)

### N'Jobu

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=47 "Edit section: N'Jobu")]

**N'Jobu** (portrayed by [Sterling K. Brown](/wiki/Sterling_K._Brown "Sterling K. Brown")) is the younger brother of T'Chaka and an agent of the War Dogs. After betraying his own people and aiding Ulysses Klaue with getting vibranium out of Wakanda with the intention of allowing oppressed people to possess its power, N'Jobu is confronted and killed by T'Chaka. His only child—his son—N'Jadaka eventually figures this out and plans to avenge his death and finish his work by becoming King.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Black Panther*.

### Nkati / The Lion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=48 "Edit section: Nkati / The Lion")]

See also: [Lion (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Lion_(Marvel_Comics) "Lion (Marvel Comics)")

**Nkati** (voiced by [Cress Williams](/wiki/Cress_Williams "Cress Williams")),[[69]](#cite_note-69)[[70]](#cite_note-70) also known as **The Lion**, is a former Wakandan general turned warlord and pirate in the [13th century BC](/wiki/13th_century_BC "13th century BC"). By 1260 BC, he had managed to use the advanced technology he stole from the country to found his own tyrannical kingdom.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *Eyes of Wakanda*.

### Jackson Norriss

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: Jackson Norriss")]

See also: [Nighthawk (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Nighthawk_(Marvel_Comics) "Nighthawk (Marvel Comics)")

**Jackson Norriss** (portrayed by [Scoot McNairy](/wiki/Scoot_McNairy "Scoot McNairy")) is a member of the [Ten Rings](/wiki/Ten_Rings_(organization) "Ten Rings (organization)") who was ordered by [Xu Wenwu](/wiki/Xu_Wenwu "Xu Wenwu") to bring [Trevor Slattery](/wiki/Trevor_Slattery "Trevor Slattery") to him, so he could be punished for mocking his persona. Norriss disguised himself as a documentary filmmaker and interviewed Slattery at the [Seagate Prison](/wiki/Seagate_Prison "Seagate Prison"), before revealing himself and abducting Slattery.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the [Marvel One-Shot](/wiki/Marvel_One-Shots "Marvel One-Shots") *[All Hail the King](/wiki/All_Hail_the_King "All Hail the King")* (2014).

### Cassandra Nova

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=50 "Edit section: Cassandra Nova")]

See also: [Cassandra Nova](/wiki/Cassandra_Nova "Cassandra Nova")

**Cassandra Nova** (portrayed by [Emma Corrin](/wiki/Emma_Corrin "Emma Corrin")) is a mutant with [telekinetic](/wiki/Telekinetic "Telekinetic") and [telepathic](/wiki/Telepathic "Telepathic") powers who is the twin sister of [Charles Xavier](/wiki/Charles_Xavier_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Charles Xavier (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). She was banished to the [Void](/wiki/Void_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe_location) "Void (Marvel Cinematic Universe location)") by the [Time Variance Authority](/wiki/Time_Variance_Authority_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Deadpool & Wolverine*.

## O

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=51 "Edit section: O")]

### Kraglin Obfonteri

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=52 "Edit section: Kraglin Obfonteri")]

See also: [Kraglin](/wiki/Kraglin "Kraglin")

**Kraglin Obfonteri** (portrayed by [Sean Gunn](/wiki/Sean_Gunn "Sean Gunn")) is a [Xandarian](/wiki/Xandar_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Xandar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and the first mate of Yondu Udonta's faction of the Ravagers. In 2014, following the mutiny of the other Ravagers, Kraglin remains loyal to Yondu and helps rescue the Guardians of the Galaxy from Ego. After Yondu's death, Kraglin acquires a similar cybernetic fin and Yaka arrow. In 2023, he is transported to Earth to join the Avengers and other heroes in the battle against a past version of Thanos. He later joins the Guardians as an official member.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in six projects: the films *[Guardians of the Galaxy](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(film) "Guardians of the Galaxy (film)")* (2014), *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2*, *Avengers: Endgame* (cameo), *Thor: Love and Thunder*, and *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3*; and the Disney+ special *[The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special](/wiki/The_Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Holiday_Special "The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special")* (2022). An alternate universe variant of Kraglin appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[67]](#cite_note-WhatIfSDCC-67)

### Cull Obsidian

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=53 "Edit section: Cull Obsidian")]

See also: [Black Dwarf (comics)](/wiki/Black_Dwarf_(comics) "Black Dwarf (comics)")

**Cull Obsidian** (voiced and portrayed via motion capture by [Terry Notary](/wiki/Terry_Notary "Terry Notary")) is an adopted son of [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") equipped with a chain hammer and an arm blade. In 2018, he and [Ebony Maw](#Ebony_Maw) go to Earth to retrieve the [Time Stone](/wiki/Time_Stone "Time Stone"). They arrive in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and find themselves confronted by [Stephen Strange](/wiki/Stephen_Strange_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), [Wong](/wiki/Wong_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wong (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), [Tony Stark](/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and later [Peter Parker](/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). In the ensuing fight, Obsidian is tricked by Wong into jumping through a portal into Antarctica, severing his hand in the process. He is rescued by [Proxima Midnight](/wiki/Proxima_Midnight_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Proxima Midnight (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Corvus Glaive](/wiki/Corvus_Glaive_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Corvus Glaive (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and his hand is replaced by a cybernetic replacement. He then partakes in the battle in [Wakanda](/wiki/Wakanda_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), killing many people and knocking [James Rhodes](/wiki/James_Rhodes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") out of the sky. Later, Obsidian attacks [Vision](/wiki/Vision_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and is confronted by [Bruce Banner](/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), who defeats him in the [Hulkbuster armor](/wiki/Iron_Man%27s_armor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#Hulkbuster_armor "Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") by sending him flying into the Wakandan energy shield, incinerating Obsidian on impact.

An alternate version of Cull Obsidian from 2014 arrives with an alternate Thanos and his army into the main universe. During the battle, he is stepped on and killed by [Scott Lang](/wiki/Scott_Lang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Avengers: Infinity War*. Alternate universe variants of Obsidian appeared in two projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Otto Octavius / Doctor Octopus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=54 "Edit section: Otto Octavius / Doctor Octopus")]

See also: [Doctor Octopus](/wiki/Doctor_Octopus "Doctor Octopus") and [Otto Octavius (film character)](/wiki/Otto_Octavius_(film_character) "Otto Octavius (film character)")

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) alternate universe variants of **Otto Octavius**, a former [Oscorp](/wiki/Oscorp_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Oscorp (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") scientist with four mechanical tentacles fused to his back who became a criminal known as **Doctor Octopus**, have appeared in two projects: the film *Spider-Man: No Way Home* (portrayed by [Alfred Molina](/wiki/Alfred_Molina "Alfred Molina")); and the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man* (voiced by [Hugh Dancy](/wiki/Hugh_Dancy "Hugh Dancy")).[[72]](#cite_note-FYGGDO-72)[[34]](#cite_note-101CopyrightCastYFNSM-34) Molina reprises his role from Sam Raimi's film *[Spider-Man 2](/wiki/Spider-Man_2 "Spider-Man 2")* (2004).

### Thor Odinson

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=55 "Edit section: Thor Odinson")]

Main article: [Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Thor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

### Okoye

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=56 "Edit section: Okoye")]

See also: [Okoye (character)](/wiki/Okoye_(character) "Okoye (character)")

**Okoye** (portrayed by [Danai Gurira](/wiki/Danai_Gurira "Danai Gurira")) is a Wakandan warrior and the former general of the Dora Milaje. In 2016, she fights on [T'Challa](/wiki/T%27Challa_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s side when N'Jadaka plots to usurp the throne. In 2018, she fights alongside members of the [Avengers](/wiki/Avengers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to combat [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and his army during the battle in Wakanda. She survives [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip") and helps the remaining Avengers for the next five years. In 2023, she participates in the battle against an alternate version of Thanos and his army. In 2024, she attends T'Challa's funeral. In 2025, she and Shuri go to Cambridge, Massachusetts to meet an MIT student, [Riri Williams](#Riri_Williams_/_Ironheart), who built a vibranium-detecting machine and plans to take her to Wakanda. Later, she was removed from her post as a General as she could not protect Shuri and Riri who were kidnapped by Namor. Later Shuri bestows her with the Midnight Angel Armor, recruiting her and Aneka to fight alongside the rest of the Wakandan military forces against Namor and his Talokan army. Okoye then continues to support Wakanda from overseas, breaking Everett Ross out of a prison transport.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *Black Panther*, *Avengers: Infinity War*, *Avengers: Endgame*, and *Black Panther: Wakanda Forever*. Alternate universe variants of Okoye appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* (voiced by Gurira in the first season and Kenna Ramsey in the third season)[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10): 2 and *Marvel Zombies* (voiced by Ramsey).[[73]](#cite_note-73)

### Stakar Ogord

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=57 "Edit section: Stakar Ogord")]

See also: [Starhawk (character)](/wiki/Starhawk_(character) "Starhawk (character)")

**Stakar Ogord** (portrayed by [Sylvester Stallone](/wiki/Sylvester_Stallone "Sylvester Stallone")) is a legendary Ravager captain and the leader of the Stakar Ravager Clan. Ogord saved Yondu Udonta from a life of slavery to the Kree and welcomed him to the Ravagers, but was later forced to exile him for engaging in child trafficking, a violation of the Ravager code. Ogord joins the other Ravager clans at honoring Yondu during his funeral after his sacrifice to save Peter Quill. In a mid-credits scene, Stakar and Martinex reunite with their old teammates Charlie-27, Aleta Ogord, Mainframe, and Krugarr. He later aids the Guardians in their infiltration of the Orgoscope space station during their search of a passkey to disable the kill switch in Rocket's heart and save his life.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2* and *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3*.

### Harry Osborn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=58 "Edit section: Harry Osborn")]

See also: [Harry Osborn](/wiki/Harry_Osborn "Harry Osborn")

**Harold** "**Harry**" **Osborn** (voiced by [Zeno Robinson](/wiki/Zeno_Robinson "Zeno Robinson"))[[34]](#cite_note-101CopyrightCastYFNSM-34) is an [influencer](/wiki/Influencer "Influencer") and the son of [Norman Osborn](#Norman_Osborn_/_Green_Goblin) from an alternate universe who befriends [Peter Parker](/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and provides him support as Spider-Man's "dude at the desk".

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man*.

### Norman Osborn / Green Goblin

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=59 "Edit section: Norman Osborn / Green Goblin")]

See also: [Norman Osborn](/wiki/Norman_Osborn "Norman Osborn"), [Green Goblin](/wiki/Green_Goblin "Green Goblin"), and [Norman Osborn (2002 film series character)](/wiki/Norman_Osborn_(2002_film_series_character) "Norman Osborn (2002 film series character)")

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) alternate universe variants of **Norman Osborn**, a scientist and the CEO of [Oscorp](/wiki/Oscorp_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Oscorp (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") with an insane split personality known as the **Green Goblin** who uses advanced armor and equipment, have appeared in two projects: the film *Spider-Man: No Way Home* (portrayed by [Willem Dafoe](/wiki/Willem_Dafoe "Willem Dafoe")); and the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man* (voiced by [Colman Domingo](/wiki/Colman_Domingo "Colman Domingo")).[[74]](#cite_note-74) Dafoe reprises his role from Sam Raimi's [*Spider-Man* film series](/wiki/Spider-Man_(2002_film_series) "Spider-Man (2002 film series)").

### The Other

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=60 "Edit section: The Other")]

**The Other** (portrayed by [Alexis Denisof](/wiki/Alexis_Denisof "Alexis Denisof")) is a character from the MCU and the grim leader of an alien race called the [Chitauri](/wiki/Chitauri_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Chitauri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). He is a servant of Thanos and uses telepathic powers. In 2014, he is killed by [Ronan the Accuser](#Ronan_the_Accuser).

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *[The Avengers](/wiki/The_Avengers_(2012_film) "The Avengers (2012 film)")* (2012) and *Guardians of the Galaxy*.[[75]](#cite_note-75)[[76]](#cite_note-76)

### Ouroboros "O.B." / A.D. Doug

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=61 "Edit section: Ouroboros \"O.B.\" / A.D. Doug")]

See also: [Mr. Ouroboros](/wiki/Mr._Ouroboros "Mr. Ouroboros")

**Ouroboros** "**O.B.**"[[77]](#cite_note-Ouroboros_O.B.-77)[[78]](#cite_note-78) (portrayed by [Ke Huy Quan](/wiki/Ke_Huy_Quan "Ke Huy Quan")), is the chief engineer of the [Time Variance Authority](/wiki/Time_Variance_Authority_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), who deals with the maintenance and repair of all the complex tools, including time travel devices. In "Science/Fiction," O.B. is revealed to be a temporal variant of Dr. **A.D. Doug**, PhD, a failed science fiction author and theoretical physics teacher at Caltech in 1994 who would rather focus on his writing than on science.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the Disney+ series *Loki*; and the comic book series *TVA*.[[40]](#cite_note-BlairTVAMarvelPodcast-40)

### Christine Palmer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=64 "Edit section: Christine Palmer")]

See also: [Christine Palmer](/wiki/Christine_Palmer "Christine Palmer")

Dr. **Christine Palmer** (portrayed by [Rachel McAdams](/wiki/Rachel_McAdams "Rachel McAdams")) is an emergency surgeon who is a colleague and former lover of Stephen Strange. She saves his life after he is nearly killed by Kaecilius. After surviving the Blip, she ends up marrying her boyfriend Charlie, who is also a big fan of Stephen Strange.

In the alternate universe designated as Earth-838, Palmer works for the [Baxter Foundation](/wiki/Baxter_Foundation_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Baxter Foundation (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to analyze [multiversal](/wiki/Multiverse_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Multiverse (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") threats.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Doctor Strange* and *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*. Alternate universe variants of Palmer appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*;[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10) and the film *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*.

### Pearl Pangan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=65 "Edit section: Pearl Pangan")]
