**Pearl Pangan** (voiced by [Cathy Ang](/wiki/Cathy_Ang "Cathy Ang"))[[8]](#cite_note-CastYFNSM-8) is the childhood crush and later classmate of [Peter Parker](/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") at [Rockford T. Bales High School](/wiki/Rockford_T._Bales_High_School "Rockford T. Bales High School") in an alternate universe.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the comic book series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man*;[[33]](#cite_note-PrequelComicYFNSM-33) and the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man*.

### Jonathan Pangborn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=66 "Edit section: Jonathan Pangborn")]

**Jonathan Pangborn** (portrayed by [Benjamin Bratt](/wiki/Benjamin_Bratt "Benjamin Bratt")) is a former Master of the Mystic Arts who was trained by the Ancient One but later chose to leave [Kamar-Taj](/wiki/Kamar-Taj_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kamar-Taj (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") as he only wanted to heal his paralysis. [Karl Mordo](#Karl_Mordo) later drains him of his magic and his ability to walk, as Mordo claims that there are too many sorcerers.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Doctor Strange*.

### Mr. Paradox

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=67 "Edit section: Mr. Paradox")]

See also: [Mr. Paradox](/wiki/Mr._Paradox "Mr. Paradox")

**Mr. Paradox** (portrayed by [Matthew Macfadyen](/wiki/Matthew_Macfadyen "Matthew Macfadyen")) is a [Time Variance Authority](/wiki/Time_Variance_Authority_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") (TVA) agent who oversees an unsanctioned project to speed up the death of the [Earth-10005](/wiki/Earth-10005 "Earth-10005") universe by using the "Time Ripper", a machine which can hasten the end of timelines. Paradox dislikes the method TVA imposes on dying timelines, viewing his Time Ripper as an act of [mercy killing](/wiki/Mercy_killing "Mercy killing"), and hopes to prove himself with this act to become the leader of the TVA. After Deadpool and Wolverine destroy the Time Ripper, which also kills Cassandra Nova, Paradox is arrested by his superior Hunter B-15.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Deadpool & Wolverine*.[[79]](#cite_note-79)

### May Parker

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=68 "Edit section: May Parker")]

See also: [Aunt May](/wiki/Aunt_May "Aunt May")

**May Parker** (portrayed by [Marisa Tomei](/wiki/Marisa_Tomei "Marisa Tomei")) is Peter Parker's aunt and [Ben Parker](/wiki/Ben_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ben Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s widow. She discovers that Peter is Spider-Man, unlike previous cinematic iterations, when she enters the room and sees him in the suit. She was one of the victims of the Blip in 2018, but she is brought back to life and shows up at Tony Stark's funeral in 2023. Following that, she starts dating [Happy Hogan](/wiki/Happy_Hogan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Happy Hogan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). When villains from different universes arrive in hers, May temporarily houses [Norman Osborn](/wiki/Norman_Osborn_(2002_film_series_character) "Norman Osborn (2002 film series character)") at the F.E.A.S.T. shelter where she works before assisting Peter in bringing the remaining displaced villains to Hogan's apartment so they can be treated. May is fatally wounded by an inflicted stab wound from being impaled by Osborn's glider when Osborn's alternate personality, the Green Goblin takes control, persuades most of the other villains to reject their cures and stages an uprising inside Hogan's apartment. She gives the grieving Peter her parting advice, "[With great power, there must also come great responsibility](/wiki/With_great_power,_there_must_also_come_great_responsibility "With great power, there must also come great responsibility")", before dying.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in five projects: the films *[Captain America: Civil War](/wiki/Captain_America:_Civil_War "Captain America: Civil War")* (2016), *Spider-Man: Homecoming*, *Avengers: Endgame* (cameo), *[Spider-Man: Far From Home](/wiki/Spider-Man:_Far_From_Home "Spider-Man: Far From Home")* (2019), and *Spider-Man: No Way Home*. An alternate universe variant of May appeared in two projects: the comic book series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man*;[[33]](#cite_note-PrequelComicYFNSM-33) and the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man* (voiced by [Kari Wahlgren](/wiki/Kari_Wahlgren "Kari Wahlgren")).[[34]](#cite_note-101CopyrightCastYFNSM-34)

**Eugene Patilio** (portrayed by Brandon Stanley),[[80]](#cite_note-80) also known as **Leap-Frog**, is the son of a rich GLK&H client who tries to become a crime fighter in a frog-themed costume.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *She-Hulk: Attorney at Law*.

### Vincent Patilio

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=71 "Edit section: Vincent Patilio")]

See also: [Leap-Frog (comics)](/wiki/Leap-Frog_(comics) "Leap-Frog (comics)")

**Vincent Patilio** (voiced by [Kellen Goff](/wiki/Kellen_Goff "Kellen Goff"))[[81]](#cite_note-81) is [Otto Octavius](#Otto_Octavius_/_Doctor_Octopus)'s assistant in an alternate universe.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man*.

### Phastos

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=72 "Edit section: Phastos")]

See also: [Phastos](/wiki/Phastos "Phastos")

**Phastos** (portrayed by [Brian Tyree Henry](/wiki/Brian_Tyree_Henry "Brian Tyree Henry")) is an Eternal and an intelligent cosmic-powered inventor who helps humanity progress technologically behind the scenes before abandoning them to live in exile following the [bombing of Hiroshima](/wiki/Atomic_bombings_of_Hiroshima_and_Nagasaki "Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki"). By 2024, he lives under the human alias of "**Phil**" with a husband named **Ben** (portrayed by [Haaz Sleiman](/wiki/Haaz_Sleiman "Haaz Sleiman")) and their 10-year-old son **Jack** (portrayed by Esai Daniel Cross).[[82]](#cite_note-82)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Eternals*.[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2)

### Todd Phelps / HulkKing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=73 "Edit section: Todd Phelps / HulkKing")]

"Todd Phelps" redirects here. For the political candidate, see [2016 United States House of Representatives elections in Texas § District 21](/wiki/2016_United_States_House_of_Representatives_elections_in_Texas#District_21 "2016 United States House of Representatives elections in Texas").

**Todd Phelps** (portrayed by [Jon Bass](/wiki/Jon_Bass_(actor) "Jon Bass (actor)")), also known by his online username "**HulkKing"**, is a billionaire and the founder and leader of [Intelligencia](/wiki/Intelligencia_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Intelligencia (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). He made plans to obtain She-Hulk's DNA through different minions. During the confrontation at a seminar hosted by Abomination, Phelps injected himself with the DNA and became a Hulk as Titania and Hulk also showed up. The transformation was undone when She-Hulk filed a complaint about it and her cousin's unexplained appearance to [K.E.V.I.N.](/wiki/K.E.V.I.N. "K.E.V.I.N.") Todd and those involved are arrested as She-Hulk plans to see Todd in court.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *She-Hulk: Attorney at Law*.

### Chester Phillips

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=74 "Edit section: Chester Phillips")]

See also: [Chester Phillips](/wiki/Chester_Phillips "Chester Phillips")

[Colonel](/wiki/Colonel_(United_States) "Colonel (United States)") **Chester Phillips** (portrayed by [Tommy Lee Jones](/wiki/Tommy_Lee_Jones "Tommy Lee Jones")) is one of the founders of S.H.I.E.L.D., along with [Peggy Carter](/wiki/Peggy_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Howard Stark](#Howard_Stark).[[83]](#cite_note-83) He leads the [Strategic Scientific Reserve](/wiki/Strategic_Scientific_Reserve "Strategic Scientific Reserve") during World War II and is initially skeptical of [Abraham Erskine](/wiki/Abraham_Erskine_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Abraham Erskine (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s choice to administer the [Super Soldier Serum](/wiki/Super_Soldier_Serum_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Super Soldier Serum (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to Steve Rogers, but later has a change of heart after witnessing Rogers's heroic actions.[[84]](#cite_note-84)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Captain America: The First Avenger*. An alternate universe variant of Phillips appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Phyla

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=75 "Edit section: Phyla")]

See also: [Phyla-Vell](/wiki/Phyla-Vell "Phyla-Vell")

**Phyla** (portrayed by [Kai Zen](/wiki/Kai_Zen_(actress) "Kai Zen (actress)")) is a Star Child, a species genetically engineered by the [High Evolutionary](/wiki/High_Evolutionary_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "High Evolutionary (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[85]](#cite_note-85) Phyla met the [Guardians of the Galaxy](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and escaped her imprisonment, joining the new lineup of the Guardians, led by [Rocket Raccoon](/wiki/Rocket_Raccoon_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Rocket Raccoon (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3*.[[86]](#cite_note-86)

### Alexander Pierce

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=76 "Edit section: Alexander Pierce")]

See also: [Alexander Pierce](/wiki/Alexander_Pierce "Alexander Pierce")

**Alexander Pierce** (portrayed by [Robert Redford](/wiki/Robert_Redford "Robert Redford")) is the secretary of the World Security Council and the secret director of Hydra operating within S.H.I.E.L.D. He plans on using Project Insight to eliminate individuals that would oppose or threaten Hydra goals, those who are recognized as a threat to Hydra based on [Arnim Zola](#Arnim_Zola)'s algorithm. When Pierce learns that [Nick Fury](/wiki/Nick_Fury_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") is investigating Project Insight's confidential files, he dispatches the [Winter Soldier](/wiki/Bucky_Barnes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to eliminate him and Steve Rogers. However, Pierce's plan is foiled by Rogers, Natasha Romanoff, Sam Wilson, and S.H.I.E.L.D. loyalists before Pierce is killed by Fury.

In 2023, the Avengers time travel to 2012, where Pierce attempts to take custody of Loki and the Tesseract away from Tony Stark and Thor following the [Battle of New York](/wiki/Battle_of_New_York_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[87]](#cite_note-Screen_Rant-87)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Captain America: The Winter Soldier*. An alternate universe variant of Pierce appeared in one project: the film *Avengers: Endgame*.

**Pip the Troll** (voiced by [Patton Oswalt](/wiki/Patton_Oswalt "Patton Oswalt")) is an ally and friend of [Eros](/wiki/Eros_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Eros (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Eternals* (mid-credits cameo).[[88]](#cite_note-StylesOswalt-88)

### Cherry Pitts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=78 "Edit section: Cherry Pitts")]

**Clark** "**Cherry**" **Pitts** (portrayed by [Clark Johnson](/wiki/Clark_Johnson "Clark Johnson")) is a retired [New York Police Department](/wiki/New_York_Police_Department "New York Police Department") (NYPD) officer who works as an investigator at the [Murdock & McDuffie](/wiki/Murdock_%26_McDuffie "Murdock & McDuffie") law firm and is aware of [Matt Murdock](/wiki/Matt_Murdock_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Matt Murdock (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s secret identity as the vigilante Daredevil.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Daredevil: Born Again*.

Main article: [Benjamin Poindexter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Benjamin_Poindexter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Benjamin Poindexter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) **Benjamin** "**Dex**" **Poindexter** (portrayed by [Wilson Bethel](/wiki/Wilson_Bethel "Wilson Bethel")), a psychopathic assassin and former [FBI](/wiki/FBI "FBI") agent known as **Bullseye** who is a highly skilled [marksman](/wiki/Marksman "Marksman") capable of using almost any object as a lethal projectile, has appeared in two projects: the Marvel Television series *Daredevil*;[[89]](#cite_note-89) and the Disney+ series *Daredevil: Born Again*.[[90]](#cite_note-90) An alternate universe variant of Bullseye (portrayed by Curtis Small) appeared in one project: the film *Deadpool & Wolverine*.[[91]](#cite_note-91)[[92]](#cite_note-Deadpool&WolverineCameos-92)

### Hank Pym / Ant-Man

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=82 "Edit section: Hank Pym / Ant-Man")]

See also: [Hank Pym](/wiki/Hank_Pym "Hank Pym")

Dr. **Henry "Hank" Pym** (portrayed by [Michael Douglas](/wiki/Michael_Douglas "Michael Douglas")) is an [entomologist](/wiki/Entomology "Entomology") and [quantum physicist](/wiki/Quantum_mechanics "Quantum mechanics") who created the formula for the [Pym Particle](/wiki/Pym_Particles_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Pym Particles (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), a [subatomic particle](/wiki/Subatomic_particle "Subatomic particle") that changes the distance between atoms, allowing one to shrink and grow in relative size, while increasing strength. Hank Pym is the MCU's original **Ant-Man**, a role begun in 1963, during his tenure as a high-ranking scientist and operative at S.H.I.E.L.D.

As the Ant-Man, Hank Pym operated as a classified agent performing field missions on behalf of S.H.I.E.L.D., using a self-designed shrinking suit powered by Pym particles that also gave him the ability to communicate with different species of ants by producing [electromagnetic waves](/wiki/Electromagnetic_radiation "Electromagnetic radiation") that stimulate the [olfactory nerve](/wiki/Olfactory_nerve "Olfactory nerve") centers of the ants. During one of these classified missions in 1987, his wife [Janet van Dyne](#Janet_van_Dyne_/_Wasp) was lost within the [Quantum Realm](/wiki/Quantum_Realm "Quantum Realm") and presumed dead. Following the incident, Pym resigns from S.H.I.E.L.D. in 1989, after he suspects Howard Stark of attempting to replicate the Pym particle formula. Pym retires the Ant-Man suit and founds his own technology company in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco"). In 2015, after [Darren Cross](/wiki/Darren_Cross_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Darren Cross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")' takeover of the company, Pym subsequently recruits Scott Lang to take on the mantle of Ant-Man, with the assistance of his estranged daughter [Hope van Dyne](/wiki/Hope_van_Dyne "Hope van Dyne"). Together, they prevent Cross from selling the Yellowjacket technology to Hydra. In 2016, Pym inadvertently violates the [Sokovia Accords](/wiki/Sokovia_Accords "Sokovia Accords") because of Lang's misuse of his technology, and becomes a fugitive along with Hope from the [FBI](/wiki/Federal_Bureau_of_Investigation "Federal Bureau of Investigation"). In 2018, with help from Lang and Hope, he subsequently succeeds in rescuing Janet from the Quantum Realm. However, shortly after, Pym, along with Janet and Hope, becomes a victim of [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip"). In 2023, Pym is restored to life and attends [Tony Stark](/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s funeral with Janet, Hope, and Lang. In 2026, he, Janet, Hope, Lang, and [Cassie Lang](/wiki/Cassie_Lang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Cassie Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") are accidentally transported to the Quantum Realm where they encounter and seemingly defeat and prevent [Kang the Conqueror](/wiki/Kang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") from escaping into the [multiverse](/wiki/Multiverse_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Multiverse (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

In an [alternate universe](/wiki/Avengers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#Reversing_the_Blip "Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Pym is working at Camp Lehigh, when vials of Pym particles are stolen from him by Steve Rogers. In another [alternate universe](/wiki/What_If..._the_World_Lost_Its_Mightiest_Heroes%3F "What If... the World Lost Its Mightiest Heroes?"), Pym dresses in the **Yellowjacket** armor and murders prospective Avengers Tony Stark, Thor, Clint Barton, Bruce Banner, and Natasha Romanoff as revenge on Nick Fury for Hope's death. He is eventually arrested and taken to Asgard by Loki for killing Thor. In [another alternate universe](/wiki/What_If..._Zombies%3F! "What If... Zombies?!"), Pym ventures into the Quantum Realm and is infected by Janet with a zombie virus that is subsequently spread across Earth when they return. Later in the battle for "Infinity Hulk", the zombified Pym is defeated by Peter Parker and Scott Lang, and sliced in half by Blade Knight. In another alternate universe, Pym, still serving as Ant-Man, is recruited by Howard Stark and Peggy Carter in 1988 to join [a response team](/wiki/Avengers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to address the threat posed by [Ego](/wiki/Ego_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ego (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and his son [Peter Quill](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Sympathizing with him over his loss of his mother, Pym manages to speak with Quill and aids him in defeating Ego, before adopting him.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *[Ant-Man](/wiki/Ant-Man_(film) "Ant-Man (film)")* (2015), *[Ant-Man and the Wasp](/wiki/Ant-Man_and_the_Wasp "Ant-Man and the Wasp")* (2018), *Avengers: Endgame*, and *Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania*. Alternate universe variants of Pym appeared in three projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* and *Marvel Zombies*.[[67]](#cite_note-WhatIfSDCC-67)[[93]](#cite_note-TrailerCharactersMZ-93)

#### Quill family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=85 "Edit section: Quill family")]

See also: [Meredith Quill](/wiki/Meredith_Quill "Meredith Quill") and [J'son (character)](/wiki/J%27son_(character) "J'son (character)")

**Meredith Quill** (portrayed by [Laura Haddock](/wiki/Laura_Haddock "Laura Haddock")) is the mother of [Peter Quill](/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and the ex-lover of [Ego](/wiki/Ego_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ego (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). She meets Ego, falls in love with him, and becomes pregnant with their son. She enjoys pop music, and gives Peter her [walkman](/wiki/Walkman "Walkman"), along with her mixtapes. She later dies of brain cancer, unaware that the tumor was caused by Ego. **Jason Quill** (portrayed by [Gregg Henry](/wiki/Gregg_Henry "Gregg Henry")) is the father of Meredith and grandfather of Peter.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) these characters have appeared in three projects: the films *Guardians of the Galaxy*, *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2*, and *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3*.

### Irani Rael / Nova Prime

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=88 "Edit section: Irani Rael / Nova Prime")]

See also: [Irani Rael](/wiki/Irani_Rael "Irani Rael")

**Irani Rael** (portrayed by [Glenn Close](/wiki/Glenn_Close "Glenn Close")), also known as the **Nova Prime**, is the leader of the [Nova Corps](/wiki/Nova_Corps_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nova Corps (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). In 2014, she leads the Corps to victory with the help of the Guardians of the Galaxy when Ronan the Accuser attempts to destroy her home planet of Xandar with the [Power Stone](/wiki/Power_Stone_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

An alternate version of Rael appeared in the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* where she rescued [Nebula](/wiki/Nebula_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") following Ronan the Accuser's betrayal and murder of Thanos and Gamora. However, Rael betrayed Xandar to make a deal with Ronan, only to be defeated by Nebula, falling to her death following a failed attempt to kill Nebula and escape.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Guardians of the Galaxy*. An alternate universe variant of Rael (voiced by Julianne Grossman) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Maria Rambeau

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=89 "Edit section: Maria Rambeau")]

"Binary (Marvel Cinematic Universe)" redirects here. For other uses, see [Binary (Carol Danvers)](/wiki/Binary_(Carol_Danvers) "Binary (Carol Danvers)").

Captain **Maria Rambeau** (portrayed by [Lashana Lynch](/wiki/Lashana_Lynch "Lashana Lynch")) is a former [United States Air Force](/wiki/United_States_Air_Force "United States Air Force") pilot and a single mother of [Monica Rambeau](#Monica_Rambeau). She becomes Carol Danvers's best friend, who is presumed dead for six years after a plane accident. Maria is reunited with Danvers during the Kree-Skrull War and helps Danvers to remember her early life. Later, she helps found [S.W.O.R.D.](/wiki/S.W.O.R.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "S.W.O.R.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and becomes its director until she dies of cancer in 2020.

In the alternate universe designated as Earth-838, Maria takes up the mantle of **Captain Marvel** instead of Danvers, serving as a member of the Illuminati until she is killed by Wanda Maximoff who dropped a statue on her after having her powers absorbed.

In another alternate universe, she adopted the mantle of **Binary** after gaining superhuman powers, becoming an ally to the [mutant](/wiki/Mutant_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Mutant (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") strike force known as the [X-Men](/wiki/X-Men "X-Men"). She meets [Monica Rambeau](/wiki/Monica_Rambeau "Monica Rambeau"), whom she never parented in her reality, as the X-Men member Beast recovers her following her displacement into their world, due to an averted incursion between it and Earth-616.[[94]](#cite_note-94)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Captain Marvel* and *The Marvels*. Alternate universe variants of Maria appeared in two projects: the films *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness* and *The Marvels*.[[95]](#cite_note-95)

### Monica Rambeau

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=90 "Edit section: Monica Rambeau")]

See also: [Monica Rambeau](/wiki/Monica_Rambeau "Monica Rambeau")

Captain **Monica Rambeau** (portrayed by [Akira Akbar](/wiki/Akira_Akbar "Akira Akbar") as a child and by [Teyonah Parris](/wiki/Teyonah_Parris "Teyonah Parris") as an adult) is the daughter of [Maria Rambeau](#Maria_Rambeau). As a child in 1995, she is inspired by [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and thinks highly of her. She also meets a family of [Skrulls](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), as well as the [Flerken](/wiki/Flerken "Flerken"), Goose. She grows up to become an agent of [S.W.O.R.D.](/wiki/S.W.O.R.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "S.W.O.R.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), which was founded by her mother.

In 2018, she is a victim of [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip"). In 2023, she is restored to life and learns that her mother died three years earlier. She returns to work at S.W.O.R.D. and is tasked with investigating a [missing-persons case](/wiki/Missing_person "Missing person") in Westview, [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). Upon arriving, she is sucked into [Wanda Maximoff](/wiki/Wanda_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s alternate reality (the "Hex"), and remains there under the alias **Geraldine**. However, when she remembers the real reality, she is promptly forced out by Maximoff. Outside, she continues to help S.W.O.R.D with the Westview investigation, but after she defends Maximoff several times, S.W.O.R.D's acting director [Tyler Hayward](/wiki/Tyler_Hayward "Tyler Hayward") kicks her off the investigation. She, [Darcy Lewis](/wiki/Darcy_Lewis "Darcy Lewis"), and [Jimmy Woo](#Jimmy_Woo) go rogue, and only she and Woo escape Maximoff's Hex expansion. After re-entering the Hex, her cells are rewritten and she gains superpowers, allowing her to absorb bullets and Maximoff's energy blasts. She then witnesses Maximoff fighting against [Agatha Harkness](/wiki/Agatha_Harkness_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and claiming the mantle as the [fabled](/wiki/Fable "Fable") Scarlet Witch. After the Hex is taken down, she befriends Maximoff and wishes her well when Maximoff leaves Westview. She then gets notified by a Skrull that a friend of her mother is seeking her out.

Rambeau goes to the [S.A.B.E.R.](/wiki/S.A.B.E.R. "S.A.B.E.R.") space station and works with Fury. In 2026, she is sent to investigate a jump point anomaly and learns that Danvers is investigating another one. When she touches it, she gets transported to a barren planet and is attacked by Kree soldiers. She gets transported back to the station and she and Fury return to Earth to [Jersey City](/wiki/Jersey_City "Jersey City") to meet [Kamala Khan](/wiki/Kamala_Khan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kamala Khan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") at her parents' house. Rambeau is reunited with Danvers there and explains how she got powers before having to rescue Khan from the sky. Eventually, she and Khan are transported to Tarnax IV, where a group of Skrulls live. When Danvers arrives, they are attacked by [Dar-Benn](/wiki/Dar-Benn "Dar-Benn") and help the Skrulls onto Danvers's spaceship. Rambeau speaks with Danvers on the ship and the three try to work out their entanglement. They go to Aladna and meet the Prince, who is married to Danvers. Rambeau fights against Dar-Benn when she arrives and helps pilot the ship as they leave. On another planet, she and Danvers have a heartfelt conversation and return to the S.A.B.E.R. Space station to evacuate Fury and the agents. They then fight Dar-Ben again on her ship, in which she acquires both Quantum bands and tears a hole in the universe. Rambeau, powered up by Khan and Danvers, flies into the other universe and closes the tear before passing out. She awakes in the [X-Mansion](/wiki/X-Mansion "X-Mansion") and meets [Binary](/wiki/Binary_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Binary (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), an alternate version of her mother, and [Beast](/wiki/Hank_McCoy "Hank McCoy").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Captain Marvel* and *The Marvels*; and the Disney+ series *WandaVision*.[[96]](#cite_note-96) An alternate universe variant of Rambeau appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[97]](#cite_note-WhatIfS3Cast-97)

### Ramonda

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=91 "Edit section: Ramonda")]

See also: [Ramonda (character)](/wiki/Ramonda_(character) "Ramonda (character)")

**Ramonda** (portrayed by [Angela Bassett](/wiki/Angela_Bassett "Angela Bassett")) is the [Queen Mother](/wiki/Queen_mother_(Africa) "Queen mother (Africa)") of Wakanda, wife of T'Chaka, and mother of T'Challa and Shuri. She stands by her son's side as [Queen mother](/wiki/Queen_mother "Queen mother") when he becomes King of Wakanda, but is soon forced into [exile](/wiki/Exile "Exile") when N'Jadaka seemingly-defeats T'Challa in ritual combat and seizes the throne.[[98]](#cite_note-Bassett-98) In 2018, she becomes the [Queen regnant](/wiki/Queen_regnant "Queen regnant") of Wakanda, and in 2023, she reunites with her children after they are restored to life. In 2024, her son dies of an illness and Ramonda is reinstated as Queen regnant of Wakanda. In 2025, Wakanda is attacked by the nation of [Talokan](/wiki/Talokan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Talokan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), their leader [Namor](#Namor) floods the throne room of the palace, and Ramonda drowns after saving [Riri Williams](#Riri_Williams_/_Ironheart) from the water.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Black Panther*, *Avengers: Endgame* (cameo), and *Black Panther: Wakanda Forever*.[[87]](#cite_note-Screen_Rant-87) An alternate universe variant of Ramonda appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10): 2

**Piotr Rasputin** (voiced by [Stefan Kapičić](/wiki/Stefan_Kapi%C4%8Di%C4%87 "Stefan Kapičić")), also known as **Colossus**, is a member of the X-Men from [Earth-10005](/wiki/Earth-10005 "Earth-10005") who has the mutant ability to transform his body into organic steel.

### Razor Fist

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=94 "Edit section: Razor Fist")]

See also: [Razor Fist](/wiki/Razor_Fist "Razor Fist")

**Razor Fist** (portrayed by [Florian Munteanu](/wiki/Florian_Munteanu "Florian Munteanu")) is a high-ranking Ten Rings agent with a steel blade for a hand. He is sent by [Wenwu](/wiki/Wenwu_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wenwu (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to take [Shang-Chi](/wiki/Shang-Chi_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Shang-Chi (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s pendant. Then he engages Shang-Chi when the Ten Rings target [Xialing](#Xu_Xialing)'s pendant until Wenwu breaks up the resulting conflict. Razor Fist later accompanies him and Ten Rings on the assault on [Ta Lo](/wiki/Ta_Lo "Ta Lo"). When the [Dweller-in-Darkness](/wiki/Dweller-in-Darkness_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Dweller-in-Darkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") escapes his seal, Razor Fist orders the Ten Rings into working with the Ta Lo villagers to help battle the Dweller and his minions as he replaces his blade with one made of dragon scales. When Xialing takes over the Ten Rings and restructures it, Razor Fist retains his old position.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings](/wiki/Shang-Chi_and_the_Legend_of_the_Ten_Rings "Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings")* (2021).

### Ravonna Renslayer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=95 "Edit section: Ravonna Renslayer")]

See also: [Ravonna](/wiki/Ravonna "Ravonna")

Judge **Ravonna Renslayer** (portrayed by [Gugu Mbatha-Raw](/wiki/Gugu_Mbatha-Raw "Gugu Mbatha-Raw")) is a former Minuteman for the Time Variance Authority (TVA) codenamed **Hunter A-23**[[100]](#cite_note-100) who rose from the ranks to become a respected judge. She oversees the Loki "variant" investigation.[[101]](#cite_note-LokiPressKit-101): 8 Prior to joining the TVA, she was a [vice-principal](/wiki/Vice-principal "Vice-principal") called **Rebecca Tourminet** in [Fremont, Ohio](/wiki/Fremont,_Ohio "Fremont, Ohio").[[102]](#cite_note-RenslayerRealName-102)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Loki*.

### Bob Reynolds / Sentry / Void

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=96 "Edit section: Bob Reynolds / Sentry / Void")]

See also: [Sentry (Robert Reynolds)](/wiki/Sentry_(Robert_Reynolds) "Sentry (Robert Reynolds)")

**Robert Reynolds** (portrayed by [Lewis Pullman](/wiki/Lewis_Pullman "Lewis Pullman")), also known as **Sentry**, is a superpowered individual suffering from [amnesia](/wiki/Amnesia "Amnesia") who is believed to be stronger than all of the [Avengers](/wiki/Avengers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") combined. He gained his powers from [Valentina Allegra de Fontaine](/wiki/Valentina_Allegra_de_Fontaine_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and the [O.X.E. Group](/wiki/O.X.E._Group "O.X.E. Group") after volunteering for their experimentation due to having developed a drug addiction. Initially intending for him to replace the Avengers, his poor mental health leads him to develop an [alter ego](/wiki/Alter_ego "Alter ego") known as **Void**, causing everything to start being absorbed into a growing darkness until the [Thunderbolts](/wiki/Thunderbolts_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thunderbolts (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") talk him down and help him regain control.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Thunderbolts\*](/wiki/Thunderbolts* "Thunderbolts*")* (2025).[[103]](#cite_note-103) He will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29)

**Franklin Richards** (portrayed by Ada Scott)[[104]](#cite_note-104) is the superpowered newborn son of [Reed Richards](#Reed_Richards_/_Mister_Fantastic) and [Sue Storm](#Sue_Storm_/_Invisible_Woman).

### Reed Richards / Mister Fantastic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=99 "Edit section: Reed Richards / Mister Fantastic")]

See also: [Mister Fantastic](/wiki/Mister_Fantastic "Mister Fantastic")

Dr. **Reed Richards** (portrayed by [Pedro Pascal](/wiki/Pedro_Pascal "Pedro Pascal")), also known as **Mister Fantastic**, is a highly intelligent scientist and the leader of the [Fantastic Four](/wiki/Fantastic_Four_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Fantastic Four (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who can stretch any part of his body to great lengths.

On [Earth-838](/wiki/Earth-838 "Earth-838"), Reed (portrayed by [John Krasinski](/wiki/John_Krasinski "John Krasinski")) is a member of the Fantastic Four and the Illuminati, having been dubbed "the smartest man alive" by his peers. The Illuminati seek to pass judgment on [Earth-616](/wiki/Earth-616_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Earth-616 (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s Stephen Strange and [America Chavez](/wiki/America_Chavez_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "America Chavez (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") for their roles in the possible destruction of the multiverse. However, Reed is killed by Wanda Maximoff via shredding after Maximoff coldly muses that someone will be left alive to raise his children.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the one-shot comics *[Fantastic Four: First Steps](/wiki/Fantastic_Four:_First_Steps_(comic) "Fantastic Four: First Steps (comic)")* (2025)[[105]](#cite_note-FirstStepsComicBook-105) and *[Fantastic Four: First Foes](/wiki/Fantastic_Four:_First_Foes "Fantastic Four: First Foes")* (2026);[[106]](#cite_note-FirstFoes-106) and the film *The Fantastic Four: First Steps*.[[107]](#cite_note-FantasticFourCast-107) He will return in the films *Avengers: Doomsday* and *[Avengers: Secret Wars](/wiki/Avengers:_Secret_Wars "Avengers: Secret Wars")* (2027).[[108]](#cite_note-SDCC2024F4-108) An alternate universe variant of Reed appeared in one project: the film *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*.[[109]](#cite_note-109)

**Rintrah** (voiced by [Adam Hugill](/wiki/Adam_Hugill "Adam Hugill")) is a [minotaur](/wiki/Minotaur "Minotaur")-like being from [R'Vaal](/wiki/R%27Vaal "R'Vaal") who is a student at [Kamar-Taj](/wiki/Kamar-Taj_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kamar-Taj (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

### William Ginter Riva

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=101 "Edit section: William Ginter Riva")]

**William Ginter Riva** (portrayed by [Peter Billingsley](/wiki/Peter_Billingsley "Peter Billingsley")) is a former Stark Industries employee who is ordered by [Obadiah Stane](#Obadiah_Stane) to replicate Tony Stark's arc reactor. Years later, in 2024, he joins [Quentin Beck](/wiki/Quentin_Beck_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Quentin Beck (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s crew to wreak havoc across Europe, helping him masquerade as a superhero named Mysterio, and controlling his drones. Following Mysterio's death, he leaks the identity of Spider-Man to [J. Jonah Jameson](/wiki/J._Jonah_Jameson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "J. Jonah Jameson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and uploads a copy of Mysterio's software onto a flash drive.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *[Iron Man](/wiki/Iron_Man_(2008_film) "Iron Man (2008 film)")* (2008) and *Spider-Man: Far From Home*.[[110]](#cite_note-110)

### Sheila Rivera

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=102 "Edit section: Sheila Rivera")]

**Sheila Rivera** (portrayed by [Zabryna Guevara](/wiki/Zabryna_Guevara "Zabryna Guevara")) is [Wilson Fisk](/wiki/Wilson_Fisk_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wilson Fisk (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s mayoral campaign director who also works on his staff during his tenure as mayor of New York City.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Daredevil: Born Again*. She will return in the upcoming film *[Spider-Man: Brand New Day](/wiki/Spider-Man:_Brand_New_Day "Spider-Man: Brand New Day")* (2026).[[111]](#cite_note-111)

### Parker Robbins / The Hood

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=103 "Edit section: Parker Robbins / The Hood")]

See also: [Hood (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Hood_(Marvel_Comics) "Hood (Marvel Comics)")

**Parker Robbins** (portrayed by [Anthony Ramos](/wiki/Anthony_Ramos "Anthony Ramos")), also known as **The Hood**, is a [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") criminal and gang leader who dons [a cloak](/wiki/The_Hood%27s_cloak_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "The Hood's cloak (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") that allows him to tap into dark arts and magic.

In an [alternate universe set in the 1870s](/wiki/What_If..._1872%3F "What If... 1872?"), Robbins was the leader of a criminal organization that kidnapped and enslaved [Chinese immigrants](/wiki/Chinese_emigration "Chinese emigration") in America before being killed by [Xu Xialing](#Xu_Xialing), who took control of his organization.[[112]](#cite_note-112)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Ironheart*.[[113]](#cite_note-WrapD23Presentation-113) An alternate universe variant of Robbins appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Jack Rollins

See also: [Jack Rollins (comics)](/wiki/Jack_Rollins_(comics) "Jack Rollins (comics)")

**Jack Rollins** (portrayed by [Callan Mulvey](/wiki/Callan_Mulvey "Callan Mulvey")) is a S.H.I.E.L.D. agent secretly working for Hydra. In 2014, he was a member of the S.T.R.I.K.E. team and served as Rumlow's second-in-command and later revealed as one of the sleeper Hydra agent, participating in the uprising. In 2023, the Avengers time travel to 2012, where Rollins and Hydra agents attempt to obtain Loki's scepter, but are tricked by the present time Rogers into giving it to him instead.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Captain America: The Winter Soldier*. Alternate universe variants of Rollins appeared in two projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Natasha Romanoff / Black Widow

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=106 "Edit section: Natasha Romanoff / Black Widow")]

Main article: [Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Natasha_Romanoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

### Ronan the Accuser

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=107 "Edit section: Ronan the Accuser")]

See also: [Ronan the Accuser](/wiki/Ronan_the_Accuser "Ronan the Accuser")

**Ronan the Accuser** (portrayed by [Lee Pace](/wiki/Lee_Pace "Lee Pace")) is a [Kree](/wiki/Kree_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") fanatic whose family was killed in the Kree-[Nova](/wiki/Nova_Corps_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nova Corps (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") War. In the 1990s, Ronan actively leads the Accusers in the Kree-[Skrull](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") war. Working together with the Kree Starforce, he attempts to launch a missile strike on Earth to eliminate the Skrulls present on the planet, but is thwarted and chased off by [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). In 2014, Ronan is hired by Thanos to acquire the [Power Stone](/wiki/Power_Stone_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), with the assistance of [Nebula](/wiki/Nebula_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), daughter of Thanos. However, Ronan's quest for vengeance and power causes him to break allegiance with Thanos and he decides to use the stone himself to serve his own agenda. Ultimately that decision leads him to a battle with the [Guardians of the Galaxy](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") ending in his own death when the Guardians use the Power Stone to vaporize Ronan.

An alternate version of Ronan appears in the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* where he betrays and killed Thanos and Gamora before laying siege to Xandar which is sealed off with an impenetrable planetary shield. Five years later, Irani Rael made a deal with Ronan to surrender Xandar to him in exchange for being allowed to continue ruling it, but she is foiled by Thanos's surviving adopted daughter Nebula. Ronan's ship is caught in the closing planetary shield in the process and destroyed, killing him and ending Ronan's reign of terror.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Guardians of the Galaxy* and *Captain Marvel*. An alternate universe variant of Ronan appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Betty Ross

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=108 "Edit section: Betty Ross")]

See also: [Betty Ross](/wiki/Betty_Ross "Betty Ross")

Dr. **Elizabeth "Betty" Ross** (portrayed by [Liv Tyler](/wiki/Liv_Tyler "Liv Tyler")) is the first love interest of [Bruce Banner](/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and the daughter of [Thaddeus Ross](/wiki/Thaddeus_Ross_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thaddeus Ross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). She is a cellular biologist at Culver University who, along with Bruce, were recruited by the U.S. Army on top secret bio-tech force enhancement research that would go on to turn Banner into the Hulk. When Bruce resurfaced into her life and on the run, Betty helped him avoid the authorities. She later visits her father at the Raft when he is imprisoned after destroying the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House") as a Red Hulk.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *[The Incredible Hulk](/wiki/The_Incredible_Hulk_(film) "The Incredible Hulk (film)")* (2008) and *[Captain America: Brave New World](/wiki/Captain_America:_Brave_New_World "Captain America: Brave New World")* (2025). An alternate universe variant of Betty (voiced by Stephanie Panisello) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[114]](#cite_note-114)[[115]](#cite_note-115)

### Everett K. Ross

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=109 "Edit section: Everett K. Ross")]

See also: [Everett K. Ross](/wiki/Everett_K._Ross "Everett K. Ross")

**Everett K. Ross** (portrayed by [Martin Freeman](/wiki/Martin_Freeman "Martin Freeman")) is a [CIA](/wiki/CIA "CIA") operative. In 2016, he is tasked with regulating members of the Avengers and later tracking down Ulysses Klaue, where he takes a bullet for Nakia and is taken to [Wakanda](/wiki/Wakanda_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") for treatment. After healing, he helps prevent [vibranium](/wiki/Vibranium_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") weapons from being taken out of Wakanda as planned by Erik Killmonger. In 2025, he secretly collaborates with Shuri and Okoye in spite of their country's ongoing political conflict with the U.S., which leads to his arrest by his ex-wife and now boss, de Fontaine, but Okoye frees him. In 2026, he is captured by [Skrulls](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and held captive in Russia, but is freed by [G'iah](/wiki/G%27iah_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "G'iah (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *Captain America: Civil War*, *Black Panther*, and *Black Panther: Wakanda Forever*; and the Disney+ series *Secret Invasion*.

### Thaddeus Ross / Red Hulk

### Brock Rumlow / Crossbones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=111 "Edit section: Brock Rumlow / Crossbones")]

See also: [Crossbones (character)](/wiki/Crossbones_(character) "Crossbones (character)")

**Brock Rumlow** (portrayed by [Frank Grillo](/wiki/Frank_Grillo "Frank Grillo")) is the commander of S.H.I.E.L.D.'s counter-terrorism S.T.R.I.K.E. team who is revealed to be a double-agent for [Hydra](/wiki/Teams_and_organizations_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe#Hydra "Teams and organizations of the Marvel Cinematic Universe"). In 2014, Rumlow and his unit are tasked with hunting [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") when he refuses to disclose S.H.I.E.L.D. information to Secretary [Alexander Pierce](#Alexander_Pierce). After being exposed by Rogers as a Hydra operative, Rumlow comes into conflict with [Sharon Carter](/wiki/Sharon_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sharon Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Sam Wilson](/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in the [Triskelion](/wiki/Triskelion_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Triskelion (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") until a [Helicarrier](/wiki/Helicarrier_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Helicarrier (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") crashes into it, leaving him with severe burns and facial scars. Rumlow abandons Hydra upon recovering and begins operating as the masked mercenary **Crossbones**. In 2016, he leads a group of other mercenaries in stealing a biological weapon from an institute for infectious diseases in [Lagos](/wiki/Lagos "Lagos"), Nigeria. The Avengers intervene and Rumlow is defeated by Rogers. In a final attempt to kill Rogers, Rumlow detonates a bomb but [Wanda Maximoff](/wiki/Wanda_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") contains the blast and levitates it and Rumlow into a nearby building, killing him and dozens of Wakandan humanitarian workers.

In an [alternate universe](/wiki/Time_Heist_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Rumlow and his S.T.R.I.K.E. team attempt to secure [Loki's](/wiki/Loki_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") scepter following the Battle of New York in 2012, but are tricked by the version of Rogers from the main universe into giving it to him instead.[[116]](#cite_note-116) In an alternate universe where [Peggy Carter](/wiki/Peggy_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#What_If...? "Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") became Captain Carter, Rumlow and his S.T.R.I.K.E. team join a mission to the *[Lemurian Star](/wiki/Lemurian_Star_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Lemurian Star (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")* where the Hydra Stomper armor is found. They later protect Secretary of State [Bucky Barnes](/wiki/Bucky_Barnes "Bucky Barnes") from an assassination attempt by the brainwashed [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#Hydra_Stomper "Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). This Rumlow does not appear to be a Hydra agent as the organization had been destroyed by Steve and Bucky in 1953, and he instead genuinely works for S.H.I.E.L.D. In an alternate universe where Thor is an [only child](/wiki/Only_child "Only child"), Rumlow appeared with S.H.I.E.L.D. to deal with the party that is spreading all over Earth. He also complained about never releasing a nuke to deal with Thor. In an alternate universe where the Emergence destroyed Earth, he appears as one of the survivors and members of a resistance movement against [Quentin Beck](/wiki/Quentin_Beck_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Quentin Beck (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s regime. In an alternate universe observed by the Watcher, Crossbones is a pirate.[[117]](#cite_note-117)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Captain America: The Winter Soldier* and *Captain America: Civil War*. Alternate universe variants of Rumlow appeared in two projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[118]](#cite_note-118)

**Jack Russell** (portrayed by [Gael García Bernal](/wiki/Gael_Garc%C3%ADa_Bernal "Gael García Bernal")), also known as **Werewolf by Night**, is a monster hunter of Mexican ancestry who has been afflicted with a curse that turns him into a [werewolf](/wiki/Werewolf "Werewolf").

### Rusty / Shatterstar

**Rusty** (portrayed by [Lewis Tan](/wiki/Lewis_Tan "Lewis Tan")), also known as **Shatterstar**, is a mutant alien from [Earth-10005](/wiki/Earth-10005 "Earth-10005") who is a friend of Wade Wilson and a former X-Force member.

### Garthan Saal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=115 "Edit section: Garthan Saal")]

See also: [Supernova (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Supernova_(Marvel_Comics) "Supernova (Marvel Comics)")

**Garthan Saal** (portrayed by [Peter Serafinowicz](/wiki/Peter_Serafinowicz "Peter Serafinowicz")) is a highly respected member of the Nova Corps that held the rank of Denarian. He was killed during the Battle of Xandar, bravely attempting to keep Ronan the Accuser from destroying the entire planet.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Guardians of the Galaxy*. An alternate universe variant of Saal appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Leonard Samson

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=116 "Edit section: Leonard Samson")]

See also: [Doc Samson](/wiki/Doc_Samson "Doc Samson")

Dr. **Leonard Samson** (portrayed by [Ty Burrell](/wiki/Ty_Burrell "Ty Burrell")) is a psychiatrist who works at Culver University and dated [Betty Ross](#Betty_Ross) during the time her ex, [Bruce Banner](/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), was in hiding as a fugitive. Upon discovering that Banner has returned, Samson feels jealous and fears for Betty's safety, leading him to inform her father, General [Thaddeus Ross](/wiki/Thaddeus_Ross_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thaddeus Ross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), of Banner's whereabouts. This results in a military attack on the Culver University campus, where, after witnessing the brutality of the military operation and seeing Banner as, the Hulk, protecting Betty, Samson regrets his decision and confronts General Ross, criticizing his obsession with capturing the Hulk.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *The Incredible Hulk*.

### Sara

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=117 "Edit section: Sara")]

See also: [Sara Wolfe (comics)](/wiki/Sara_Wolfe_(comics) "Sara Wolfe (comics)")

**Sara** (portrayed by [Sheila Atim](/wiki/Sheila_Atim "Sheila Atim")) is a [Master of the Mystic Arts](/wiki/Master_of_the_Mystic_Arts "Master of the Mystic Arts").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*. An alternate universe variant of Sara appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *Marvel Zombies*.

### Eric Savin

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=118 "Edit section: Eric Savin")]

See also: [Coldblood](/wiki/Coldblood "Coldblood")

**Eric Savin** (portrayed by [James Badge Dale](/wiki/James_Badge_Dale "James Badge Dale")) is a former [Lieutenant Colonel](/wiki/Lieutenant_Colonel "Lieutenant Colonel") and [Aldrich Killian](/wiki/Aldrich_Killian_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Aldrich Killian (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s right-hand man who undergoes the [Extremis](/wiki/Extremis_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Extremis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") treatment to enhance his combat skills and give himself various fire-based powers as well as a regenerative healing factor. He turns Jack Taggart into a human bomb as part of a terrorist attack, injuring [Happy Hogan](/wiki/Happy_Hogan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Happy Hogan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in the process. Savin then leads an assault on [Tony Stark](/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s mansion and later accompanies [Ellen Brandt](/wiki/Ellen_Brandt_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ellen Brandt (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to [Tennessee](/wiki/Tennessee "Tennessee") to recover evidence involving Extremis, though Stark is able to survive both times. After [James Rhodes](/wiki/James_Rhodes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s capture, Savin poses as the [Iron Patriot](/wiki/Iron_Patriot_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Iron Patriot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to board [Air Force One](/wiki/Air_Force_One "Air Force One") to kidnap [President](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") [Matthew Ellis](/wiki/Matthew_Ellis_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Matthew Ellis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). While he succeeds in this task, Iron Man kills Savin before he can escape.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *[Iron Man 3](/wiki/Iron_Man_3 "Iron Man 3")* (2013).

### Johann Schmidt / Red Skull

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=119 "Edit section: Johann Schmidt / Red Skull")]

See also: [Red Skull](/wiki/Red_Skull "Red Skull")

**Johann Schmidt** (portrayed initially by [Hugo Weaving](/wiki/Hugo_Weaving "Hugo Weaving") and subsequently by [Ross Marquand](/wiki/Ross_Marquand "Ross Marquand")), also known as the **Red Skull**, is the head of Hydra, the [Nazi](/wiki/Nazi "Nazi") science division during World War II. Schmidt plans global dominance under his rule by finding the Tesseract and using it as a weapon against the world, including to overthrow [Adolf Hitler](/wiki/Adolf_Hitler "Adolf Hitler"). He is revealed to have subjected himself to an early version of Erskine's super-soldier formula that had permanently disfigured his face.[[120]](#cite_note-120) After being foiled by [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Schmidt is transported to the planet [Vormir](/wiki/Vormir_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vormir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") by the Tesseract, where he is cursed in a purgatory state to serve as the **Stonekeeper** and a guide to those seeking the [Soul Stone](/wiki/Soul_Stone "Soul Stone").[[121]](#cite_note-121)[[122]](#cite_note-cameo-122) In 2018, he is met by [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Gamora](/wiki/Gamora_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and witnesses Thanos sacrificing Gamora to get the Stone. Following this, he gets freed from the curse and leaves Vormir.

In an [alternate universe](/wiki/Time_Heist_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), he meets another universe's [Natasha Romanoff](/wiki/Natasha_Romanoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Clint Barton](/wiki/Clint_Barton_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") during their quest for the Soul Stone.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Captain America: The First Avenger* and *Avengers: Infinity War*. Alternate universe variants of Schmidt appeared in two projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[123]](#cite_note-123)

### Herman Schultz / Shocker

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=120 "Edit section: Herman Schultz / Shocker")]

See also: [Shocker (character)](/wiki/Shocker_(character) "Shocker (character)")

**Herman Schultz** (portrayed by [Bokeem Woodbine](/wiki/Bokeem_Woodbine "Bokeem Woodbine")),[[124]](#cite_note-Shocker-124)[[125]](#cite_note-SlashFilm-125) also known as the **Shocker**, is a former salvage worker and professional criminal. When Jackson Brice is killed, Schultz assumes the Shocker mantle and gauntlet before tracking down a weapon retrieved by Spider-Man and assisting in a weapons deal aboard the [Staten Island Ferry](/wiki/Staten_Island_Ferry "Staten Island Ferry"). The deal is ambushed by the FBI and Spider-Man, though Schultz and Toomes manage to escape. When the crew pulls their final heist on a cargo plane transporting weapons from the Avengers, Schultz is tasked with stopping Spider-Man from intervening; the former initially overpowers the web-slinger until he is distracted by [Ned Leeds](/wiki/Ned_Leeds_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), allowing Spider-Man to web Schultz onto a bus. In a [deleted scene](/wiki/Deleted_scene "Deleted scene"), students find Schultz still webbed and take photos with him before he is eventually turned over to the authorities.[[126]](#cite_note-126)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Spider-Man: Homecoming*.

### Nicholas Scratch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=121 "Edit section: Nicholas Scratch")]

See also: [Nicholas Scratch](/wiki/Nicholas_Scratch "Nicholas Scratch")

**Nicholas Scratch** (portrayed by Abel Lysenko)[[127]](#cite_note-127) is the son of [Agatha Harkness](/wiki/Agatha_Harkness_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Born in the 1750s, he is fated to die at birth. Nicholas survives infancy due to a bargain Agatha strikes with [Death](/wiki/Death_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Death (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Agatha spends the next six years raising Nicholas while killing witches, which he grows to oppose. Together, they create a children's song that would eventually become [the Ballad of the Witches' Road](/wiki/The_Ballad_of_the_Witches%27_Road "The Ballad of the Witches' Road"). Eventually, Nicholas grows sick and Death takes him, devastating Agatha.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Agatha All Along*.

### Erik Selvig

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=122 "Edit section: Erik Selvig")]

Main article: [Erik Selvig](/wiki/Erik_Selvig "Erik Selvig")

### Sersi

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=123 "Edit section: Sersi")]

See also: [Sersi](/wiki/Sersi "Sersi")

**Sersi** (portrayed by [Gemma Chan](/wiki/Gemma_Chan "Gemma Chan")) is an Eternal with an affinity for humankind who is very empathetic and has the ability to manipulate matter. She has been in love with [Ikaris](/wiki/Ikaris_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ikaris (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") for centuries and poses as a museum curator on Earth. She becomes the new leader of the Eternals following [Ajak](/wiki/Ajak_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ajak (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s death. Sersi is Chan's second role in the MCU, after portraying [Minn-Erva](#Minn-Erva) in the film *Captain Marvel*.[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Eternals*.[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2) An alternate universe variant of Sersi appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* (non-speaking cameo).[[45]](#cite_note-WhatIfSeason3Cameos-45)

### Shalla-Bal / Silver Surfer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=124 "Edit section: Shalla-Bal / Silver Surfer")]

See also: [Shalla-Bal](/wiki/Shalla-Bal "Shalla-Bal") and [Silver Surfer](/wiki/Silver_Surfer "Silver Surfer")

**Shalla-Bal** (portrayed by [Julia Garner](/wiki/Julia_Garner "Julia Garner")), also known as the **Silver Surfer**, is an alien from the utopian planet [Zenn-La](/wiki/Zenn-La_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Zenn-La (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who agreed to become [Galactus](/wiki/Galactus_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Galactus (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s metallic-skinned herald in exchange for him sparing her planet. She travels through space on a [surfboard](/wiki/Surfboard "Surfboard")-like craft in search of planets for Galactus to feed on.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *The Fantastic Four: First Steps*.[[128]](#cite_note-128) She will return in the one-shot comic *[Fantastic Four: First Foes – Shalla-Bal](/wiki/Fantastic_Four:_First_Foes_%E2%80%93_Shalla-Bal "Fantastic Four: First Foes – Shalla-Bal")* (2026).[[129]](#cite_note-129)

### Alexei Shostakov / Red Guardian

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=125 "Edit section: Alexei Shostakov / Red Guardian")]

See also: [Red Guardian (Alexei Shostakov)](/wiki/Red_Guardian_(Alexei_Shostakov) "Red Guardian (Alexei Shostakov)")

**Alexei Shostakov** (portrayed by [David Harbour](/wiki/David_Harbour "David Harbour")), also known as the **Red Guardian**, is the Russian super-soldier counterpart to Captain America and the father figure of Natasha Romanoff and [Yelena Belova](/wiki/Yelena_Belova_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Yelena Belova (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Black Widow* and *Thunderbolts\**.[[130]](#cite_note-Panaligan-2022-130) He will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29) Alternate universe variants of Shostakov appeared in three projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* and *Marvel Zombies*;[[131]](#cite_note-131)[[132]](#cite_note-SDCC2022CBR-132) and the [mixed reality](/wiki/Mixed_reality "Mixed reality") experience *[What If...? – An Immersive Story](/wiki/What_If...%3F_%E2%80%93_An_Immersive_Story "What If...? – An Immersive Story")* (2024) (voiced by [David Lodge](/wiki/David_Lodge_(voice_actor) "David Lodge (voice actor)")).[[133]](#cite_note-ImmersiveStoryGizmodo-133)[[134]](#cite_note-ImmersiveStoryCast-134)

### Shuri / Black Panther

Main article: [Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Shuri_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

### Sif

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=127 "Edit section: Sif")]

See also: [Sif (character)](/wiki/Sif_(character) "Sif (character)")

Lady **Sif** (portrayed by [Jaimie Alexander](/wiki/Jaimie_Alexander "Jaimie Alexander")) is the leading female warrior of Asgard based on [Sif](/wiki/Sif "Sif") of Norse mythology, and Thor's childhood friend and trusted ally.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in five projects: the films *[Thor](/wiki/Thor_(film) "Thor (film)")* (2011), *Thor: The Dark World*, and *Thor: Love and Thunder*; the Marvel Television series *Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.*; and the Disney+ series *Loki* (cameo).[[135]](#cite_note-135)[[136]](#cite_note-136)[[137]](#cite_note-137) Alternate universe variants of Sif appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10)

### Jasper Sitwell

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=128 "Edit section: Jasper Sitwell")]

See also: [Jasper Sitwell](/wiki/Jasper_Sitwell "Jasper Sitwell")

**Jasper Sitwell** (portrayed by [Maximiliano Hernández](/wiki/Maximiliano_Hern%C3%A1ndez "Maximiliano Hernández")) is a [Hydra](/wiki/Hydra_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") sleeper agent who posed as a [S.H.I.E.L.D.](/wiki/S.H.I.E.L.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") agent. In 2014, he discloses Hydra's information to [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), [Natasha Romanoff](/wiki/Natasha_Romanoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and [Sam Wilson](/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and for his betrayal, is subsequently killed by [Bucky Barnes](/wiki/Bucky_Barnes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[138]](#cite_note-138)[[139]](#cite_note-villain_1-139)[[140]](#cite_note-hollywoodreporter.com-140)[[141]](#cite_note-villain_2-141)

In an [alternate universe](/wiki/Time_Heist_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), Sitwell and the other agents are tricked into giving [Loki](/wiki/Loki_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s scepter to another universe's Rogers.[[122]](#cite_note-cameo-122)[[142]](#cite_note-142)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in six projects: the films *Thor*, *The Avengers*, and *Captain America: The Winter Soldier*; the Marvel One-Shots *[The Consultant](/wiki/The_Consultant_(film) "The Consultant (film)")* (2011) and *[Item 47](/wiki/Item_47 "Item 47")* (2012);[[143]](#cite_note-143) and the Marvel Television series *Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.* An alternate universe variant of Sitwell appeared in one project: the film *Avengers: Endgame*.

**Skaar** (portrayed by Wil Deusner)[[144]](#cite_note-144) is the son of [Bruce Banner](/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") from [Sakaar](/wiki/Sakaar_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sakaar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who also inherited his Hulk powers.

### Skurge / Executioner

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=130 "Edit section: Skurge / Executioner")]

See also: [Executioner (character)](/wiki/Executioner_(character) "Executioner (character)")

**Skurge** (portrayed by [Karl Urban](/wiki/Karl_Urban "Karl Urban")) served as Loki's chosen successor to [Heimdall](/wiki/Heimdall_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Heimdall (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") as gatekeeper of the [Bifrost](/wiki/Bifrost_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bifrost (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). He is later promoted to the position of **Executioner** upon Hela's return to Asgard.[[145]](#cite_note-145) Skurge is portrayed in a mostly comedic, subservient if not absent-minded fashion. Upon realizing the fate of Asgard, he changes allegiance and ultimately sacrifices himself while assisting Thor in evacuating the people of Asgard during the battle against Hela during [Ragnarok](/wiki/Ragnarok_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ragnarok (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), using his two M-16 rifles to hold off her army.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Thor: Ragnarok*. An alternate universe variant of Skurge appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[146]](#cite_note-WI7EEs-146)

**Dmitri Smerdyakov** (voiced by [Roger Craig Smith](/wiki/Roger_Craig_Smith "Roger Craig Smith")),[[8]](#cite_note-CastYFNSM-8) also known as **Chameleon**, is a Russian criminal in an alternate universe who is part of a group of Russian bank robbers. During a robbery, Dmitri and most of his accomplices are arrested, though they are later broken out of prison by [Mila Masaryk](#Mila_Masaryk_/_Unicorn). After that, Dmitri starts wearing a white mask to hide his identity.
