### Soren

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=134 "Edit section: Soren")]

**Soren** (portrayed by [Sharon Blynn](/wiki/Sharon_Blynn "Sharon Blynn") in *Captain Marvel* and *Spider-Man: Far From Home*, and Charlotte Baker in *Secret Invasion*) is a Skrull and the wife of [Talos](#Talos). She is an original character in the MCU. The couple have a daughter named G'iah (portrayed by Harriet L. Ophuls and Auden L. Ophuls). In 1995, she and other Skrull refugees are reunited with Talos. They are offered help from [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in finding a home in space. However, with no luck, they return to Earth and Soren agrees to join [Nick Fury](/wiki/Nick_Fury_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s crew. In 2024, she disguises herself as [Maria Hill](/wiki/Maria_Hill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Maria Hill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") with Talos as Fury while they help [Spider-Man](/wiki/Spider-Man "Spider-Man") and Mysterio with the [Elementals](/wiki/Elementals_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Elementals (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") until discovering Mysterio's fraudulent nature. By 2026, it is revealed that she was killed by [Gravik](/wiki/Gravik_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Gravik (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Captain Marvel* and *Spider-Man: Far From Home*; and the Disney+ series *Secret Invasion* (flashback).

### Marc Spector / Moon Knight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=135 "Edit section: Marc Spector / Moon Knight")]

Main article: [Marc Spector (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Marc_Spector_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Marc Spector (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

#### Spector family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=136 "Edit section: Spector family")]

See also: [Mrs. Spector](/wiki/Mrs._Spector "Mrs. Spector"), [Elias Spector (comics)](/wiki/Elias_Spector_(comics) "Elias Spector (comics)"), and [Shadow Knight (character)](/wiki/Shadow_Knight_(character) "Shadow Knight (character)")

Marc Spector's family play an important role in his life. His parents are **Wendy** and **Elias Spector** (portrayed by [Fernanda Andrade](/wiki/Fernanda_Andrade "Fernanda Andrade") and Rey Lucas respectively), and his young brother is **Randall** (portrayed by Claudio Fabian Contreras). Randall died in a flooded cave that he and his older brother [Marc](/wiki/Marc_Spector_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Marc Spector (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") found. Consumed by the loss of Randall, Wendy blamed Marc for Randall's death, which caused her to become abusive towards Marc, who consequently developed [dissociative identity disorder](/wiki/Dissociative_identity_disorder "Dissociative identity disorder") and created [Steven Grant](/wiki/Steven_Grant_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Steven Grant (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to protect himself from her. Elias forgave Marc for what happened to Randall and tries to reason with Wendy to no avail. At some point when Marc was a teenager, he started to leave home. Elias tried to get him to change his mind while claiming that he can get help for Wendy. By the time Marc was an adult, Wendy had died and Elias can only get a glimpse of Marc during her week-long memorial.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the characters have appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Moon Knight*.

### Sprite

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=137 "Edit section: Sprite")]

See also: [Sprite (Eternal)](/wiki/Sprite_(Eternal) "Sprite (Eternal)")

**Sprite** (portrayed by [Lia McHugh](/wiki/Lia_McHugh "Lia McHugh"))[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2) is an Eternal who can project life-like illusions. She is in a [permanent child-like state](/wiki/Puer_aeternus "Puer aeternus"), appearing as a young girl despite being thousands of years old. Sprite often uses her illusionary powers to disguise herself as an adult woman named "**Sandra**" (portrayed by [Hannah Dodd](/wiki/Hannah_Dodd_(actress) "Hannah Dodd (actress)")).[[149]](#cite_note-149) In 2024, fellow Eternal [Sersi](#Sersi) uses the remaining energy of the [Uni-Mind](/wiki/Uni-Mind_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Uni-Mind (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to turn Sprite into a human, ending her permanent child-like state and being able to grow old.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Eternals*.

### Obadiah Stane / Iron Monger

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=138 "Edit section: Obadiah Stane / Iron Monger")]

See also: [Iron Monger](/wiki/Iron_Monger "Iron Monger")

**Obadiah Stane** (portrayed by [Jeff Bridges](/wiki/Jeff_Bridges "Jeff Bridges")), also known as **Iron Monger**,[[150]](#cite_note-150) is Tony Stark's mentor after the death of Tony's father, Howard. Stane secretly seeks control of Stark Industries, being revealed that he hired terrorists to assassinate Stark and, after the failure to do so, seeks control of the arc reactor to create his own super powered exoskeleton suit, which he succeeds in after obtaining Stark's first prototype and its schematic. Stane is subsequently killed in a confrontation against Stark.[[139]](#cite_note-villain_1-139)[[141]](#cite_note-villain_2-141)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Iron Man*. Alternate universe variants of Stane (voiced by [Kiff VandenHeuvel](/wiki/Kiff_VandenHeuvel "Kiff VandenHeuvel")) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Howard Stark

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=141 "Edit section: Howard Stark")]

See also: [Howard Stark](/wiki/Howard_Stark "Howard Stark")

**Howard Stark** (portrayed by [John Slattery](/wiki/John_Slattery "John Slattery") as an elderly man and [Dominic Cooper](/wiki/Dominic_Cooper "Dominic Cooper") as a young man) is the founder of Stark Industries, one of the founding members of S.H.I.E.L.D., and the father of Tony Stark. Howard is involved with Steve Rogers's transformation into Captain America during World War II, as well as the creation of Rogers's trademark vibranium shield. Along with Peggy Carter and his assistant [Edwin Jarvis](/wiki/Edwin_Jarvis_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Edwin Jarvis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), he is instrumental in crushing the rise of the Russian terrorist organization Leviathan. In 1991, he and his wife Maria are assassinated by the brainwashed Winter Soldier under Vasily Karpov's mind control.[[153]](#cite_note-153) Slattery's portrayal of Stark borrows traits and tones from the real-life [Walt Disney](/wiki/Walt_Disney "Walt Disney") figure.[[154]](#cite_note-154)[[155]](#cite_note-155)[[156]](#cite_note-156)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in seven projects: the films *Iron Man*, *[Iron Man 2](/wiki/Iron_Man_2 "Iron Man 2")* (2010), *Captain America: The First Avenger*, *Ant-Man*, and *Captain America: Civil War*; the Marvel One-Shot *[Agent Carter](/wiki/Agent_Carter_(film) "Agent Carter (film)")* (2013); and the Marvel Television series *[Agent Carter](/wiki/Agent_Carter_(TV_series) "Agent Carter (TV series)")* (2015–2016). Alternate universe variants of Howard appeared in two projects: the film *Avengers: Endgame*; and the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[67]](#cite_note-WhatIfSDCC-67)

She was assassinated along with her husband by the brainwashed Winter Soldier under Vasily Karpov's mind control, making it appear as an accidental car crash.

**Morgan Stark** (portrayed by [Lexi Rabe](/wiki/Lexi_Rabe "Lexi Rabe")) is the four-year-old daughter of Tony Stark and Pepper Potts. Born after Thanos's universal genocide, she grows up during the five-year Blip period with half the world's population having been wiped out of existence. In a deleted scene, a grown-up Morgan (portrayed by [Katherine Langford](/wiki/Katherine_Langford "Katherine Langford")) has a conversation with her father in the [Soul World](/wiki/Soul_World_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Soul World (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") after he sacrifices himself to save the universe by using the Infinity Stones to defeat Thanos.

### Ava Starr / Ghost

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=145 "Edit section: Ava Starr / Ghost")]

See also: [Ghost (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Ghost_(Marvel_Comics) "Ghost (Marvel Comics)")

**Ava Starr** (portrayed by [Hannah John-Kamen](/wiki/Hannah_John-Kamen "Hannah John-Kamen") as an adult, RaeLynn Bratten as a child), a reimagining of the comic book character [Ghost](/wiki/Ghost_(Marvel_Comics) "Ghost (Marvel Comics)"), is a woman with invisibility and intangibility powers.[[158]](#cite_note-Breznican-158)[[159]](#cite_note-#AntManAndTheWasp_cast_list-159)[[160]](#cite_note-AMWPressKit-160) In her childhood, Starr was caught in an accident in her father's laboratory. The ensuing explosion killed both of her parents, while Starr gained the ability to become intangible since her body was left in a constant state of "molecular disequilibrium". She is recruited by scientist [Bill Foster](/wiki/Bill_Foster_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bill Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") into S.H.I.E.L.D., where she is trained and given a containment suit to better control her powers. Starr agrees to work for the organization as an assassin and spy under the codename **Ghost** in exchange for S.H.I.E.L.D.'s help in finding a way to stabilize her condition. However, she discovers that S.H.I.E.L.D. (having been taken over by Hydra) has no intention of helping her and subsequently goes rogue to find a way to cure herself with Foster's help. The two later plan to harness the energy that Janet van Dyne's body absorbed from the Quantum Realm, putting Ghost in direct conflict with Hank Pym, Hope van Dyne, and Scott Lang. Ultimately, Janet willingly uses some of her energy to partially stabilize Starr's condition, and she departs with Foster as Janet's group vows to collect more energy for her. Starr later becomes a mercenary for Valentina Allegra de Fontaine, and eventually teams up with Yelena Belova and John Walker once a mission where they were sent to kill each other makes them realize Valentina is trying to get rid of them.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Ant-Man and the Wasp* and *Thunderbolts\**.[[130]](#cite_note-Panaligan-2022-130) She will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29) An alternate universe variant of Starr appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *Marvel Zombies*.[[132]](#cite_note-SDCC2022CBR-132)

#### Starr family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=146 "Edit section: Starr family")]

See also: [Egghead (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Egghead_(Marvel_Comics) "Egghead (Marvel Comics)")

**Elihas Starr** (portrayed by [Michael Cerveris](/wiki/Michael_Cerveris "Michael Cerveris")) and his wife **Catherine** (portrayed by [Riann Steele](/wiki/Riann_Steele "Riann Steele")) are [Ava Starr](#Ava_Starr_/_Ghost)'s parents. They died during the experiment that gave Ava her powers.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) these characters have appeared in one project: the film *Ant-Man and the Wasp*.

### Stern

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=147 "Edit section: Stern")]

Senator **Stern** (portrayed by [Garry Shandling](/wiki/Garry_Shandling "Garry Shandling")) is an original character in the MCU, named after [Howard Stern](/wiki/Howard_Stern "Howard Stern") according to *Iron Man 2* director [Jon Favreau](/wiki/Jon_Favreau "Jon Favreau").[[161]](#cite_note-2010-05-17_LA_Times-161) He is a [United States senator](/wiki/United_States_senator "United States senator") who wants [Tony Stark's armor](/wiki/Iron_Man%27s_armor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to be handed to the [U.S. government](/wiki/Federal_government_of_the_United_States "Federal government of the United States"). He harbors a strong dislike for [Stark](/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), even after handing him and James Rhodes the [Medal of Honor](/wiki/Medal_of_Honor "Medal of Honor"). He is later revealed to be affiliated with [Hydra](/wiki/Hydra_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and later arrested by the [Federal Bureau of Investigation](/wiki/Federal_Bureau_of_Investigation "Federal Bureau of Investigation") for his collusion therewith.[[162]](#cite_note-162)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Iron Man 2* and *Captain America: The Winter Soldier*.

### Samuel Sterns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=148 "Edit section: Samuel Sterns")]

See also: [Leader (character)](/wiki/Leader_(character) "Leader (character)")

Dr. **Samuel Sterns** (portrayed by [Tim Blake Nelson](/wiki/Tim_Blake_Nelson "Tim Blake Nelson")) is a gifted biologist who tries to cure Bruce Banner under the alias "Mr. Blue". After replicating Banner's blood, he then is forced to use it on Emil Blonsky. When Blonsky is transformed into the Abomination, Sterns's lab is destroyed and part of the Hulk's blood drops into an open wound on Sterns's head, with his skull mutating as he grins maniacally. The mutation grants him superhuman intelligence. Following the Harlem incident, Thaddeus Ross has Sterns imprisoned at Camp Echo One, and publicly blames him for the Abomination's actions. Instead of having the gamma radiation removed from Sterns' blood, Ross has the dosage increased. Ross promises to release Sterns if he helps advance him to the presidency. During that time, Sterns developed gamma radiation pills to prolong Ross' life after the latter becomes diagnosed with [heart failure](/wiki/Heart_failure "Heart failure"), but Ross refuses to release Sterns for fear that he would no longer make the pills. Sterns then takes revenge on Ross by increasing the doses of gamma radiation in the pills to eventually turn Ross into a Hulk. After Ross is elected to the presidency, Sterns orchestrates several attacks on his administration in a covert effort to destroy his legacy. After his operations are stopped by Sam Wilson and Joaquin Torres, he voluntarily locates Wilson to surrender himself, leading to an imprisonment in [the Raft](/wiki/The_Raft_(MCU) "The Raft (MCU)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *The Incredible Hulk* and *Captain America: Brave New World*.[[163]](#cite_note-163) Outside of the films, he is mentioned in the MCU tie-in comic *[Fury's Big Week](/wiki/Fury%27s_Big_Week "Fury's Big Week")*, which reveals him to be insane and imprisoned by [S.H.I.E.L.D.](/wiki/S.H.I.E.L.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")[[164]](#cite_note-Dr._Samuel_Sterns-164)

### Johnny Storm / Human Torch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=149 "Edit section: Johnny Storm / Human Torch")]

See also: [Human Torch](/wiki/Human_Torch "Human Torch")

**Jonathan** "**Johnny**" **Storm** (portrayed by [Joseph Quinn](/wiki/Joseph_Quinn "Joseph Quinn")), also known as the **Human Torch**, is a member of the [Fantastic Four](/wiki/Fantastic_Four_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Fantastic Four (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who can [control fire](/wiki/Pyrokinesis "Pyrokinesis") and fly.

A variant of Storm (portrayed by [Chris Evans](/wiki/Chris_Evans_(actor) "Chris Evans (actor)")) is part of the rebellion of variants that fight against [Cassandra Nova](/wiki/Cassandra_Nova_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Cassandra Nova (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in the Void. He welcomes [Deadpool](/wiki/Wade_Wilson_(film_character)#Original_timeline "Wade Wilson (film character)") and [Wolverine](/wiki/Logan_(film_character) "Logan (film character)") to the Void when they first arrive but is defeated by [Pyro](/wiki/John_Allerdyce_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "John Allerdyce (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who takes away his flames. He is captured by Nova's henchmen and taken to their hideout where she kills him by stripping away his skin and muscles, after Deadpool reveals how Storm insulted Nova behind her back.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the one-shot comics *Fantastic Four: First Steps* and *Fantastic Four: First Foes*;[[105]](#cite_note-FirstStepsComicBook-105)[[106]](#cite_note-FirstFoes-106) and the film *The Fantastic Four: First Steps*.[[165]](#cite_note-165) He will return in the films *Avengers: Doomsday* and *Avengers: Secret Wars*.[[108]](#cite_note-SDCC2024F4-108) An alternate universe variant of Johnny appeared in one project: the film *Deadpool & Wolverine*. Evans reprises his role from the 20th Century Fox films *[Fantastic Four](/wiki/Fantastic_Four_(2005_film) "Fantastic Four (2005 film)")* (2005) and *[Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer](/wiki/Fantastic_Four:_Rise_of_the_Silver_Surfer "Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer")* (2007).[[166]](#cite_note-166)

### Sue Storm / Invisible Woman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=150 "Edit section: Sue Storm / Invisible Woman")]

See also: [Invisible Woman](/wiki/Invisible_Woman "Invisible Woman")

**Susan** "**Sue**" **Storm** (portrayed by [Vanessa Kirby](/wiki/Vanessa_Kirby "Vanessa Kirby")), also known as the **Invisible Woman**, is a member of the Fantastic Four who can generate force fields and turn herself and others invisible.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the one-shot comics *Fantastic Four: First Steps* and *Fantastic Four: First Foes*;[[105]](#cite_note-FirstStepsComicBook-105)[[106]](#cite_note-FirstFoes-106) and the film *The Fantastic Four: First Steps*.[[107]](#cite_note-FantasticFourCast-107) She will return in the films *Avengers: Doomsday* and *Avengers: Secret Wars*.[[108]](#cite_note-SDCC2024F4-108)

### Wolfgang von Strucker

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=152 "Edit section: Wolfgang von Strucker")]

See also: [Baron Strucker](/wiki/Baron_Strucker "Baron Strucker")

Baron **Wolfgang von Strucker** (portrayed by [Thomas Kretschmann](/wiki/Thomas_Kretschmann "Thomas Kretschmann") as an adult and by Joey Defore as a teenager) is a high-ranking Hydra leader who specializes in [human experimentation](/wiki/Human_subject_research "Human subject research"). Strucker supervised the successful experimentation on the twins Pietro and Wanda Maximoff, where they acquired powers from the Mind Stone within Loki's scepter. Captured by the Avengers in Sokovia and taken under the custody of [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO"), Strucker is later killed by Ultron in his cell to serve as a message to the Avengers.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Captain America: The Winter Soldier* (mid-credits cameo) and *Avengers: Age of Ultron*;[[167]](#cite_note-Couto-167) and the Marvel Television series *Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.*

### Supreme Intelligence

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=153 "Edit section: Supreme Intelligence")]

See also: [Supreme Intelligence](/wiki/Supreme_Intelligence "Supreme Intelligence")

The **Supreme Intelligence** (portrayed by [Annette Bening](/wiki/Annette_Bening "Annette Bening")) is an [artificial intelligence](/wiki/Artificial_intelligence "Artificial intelligence") that is the ruler of the [Kree](/wiki/Kree_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").[[168]](#cite_note-168) The Supreme Intelligence takes the physical form of the individual most respected by whoever is speaking to it when the person is hooked up to it. To [Vers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), it takes the form of Dr. [Wendy Lawson](#Mar-Vell_/_Wendy_Lawson) (also portrayed by Bening), though Vers was initially unaware of who Lawson is due to her [amnesia](/wiki/Amnesia "Amnesia").[[169]](#cite_note-169) A deleted scene from *Captain Marvel* had the Supreme Intelligence assuming the form of [Yon-Rogg](#Yon-Rogg) (portrayed by [Jude Law](/wiki/Jude_Law "Jude Law")) when chastising him, reflecting his [ego](/wiki/Egotism "Egotism").[[170]](#cite_note-170) In 1995, the Supreme Intelligence sends Vers on a mission to Torfa to extract an undercover Kree operative, but the mission backfires and Vers is captured by a [Skrull](/wiki/Skrulls_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") faction led by [Talos](/wiki/Talos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Talos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). After Vers regains her memories and learns her true identity, she fights the Supreme Intelligence before defeating the [Starforce](/wiki/Starforce_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Starforce (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Captain Marvel* and *The Marvels* (non-speaking cameo).

### Surtur

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=154 "Edit section: Surtur")]

See also: [Surtur (character)](/wiki/Surtur_(character) "Surtur (character)")

**Surtur** (motion-captured by [Taika Waititi](/wiki/Taika_Waititi "Taika Waititi") and voiced by [Clancy Brown](/wiki/Clancy_Brown "Clancy Brown")) is a Fire Demon, lord of Muspelheim, and a significant figure in the prophecy of Ragnarok as the one who would initiate the fall of Asgard. He imprisons Thor in his lair in Muspelheim and reveals that Odin is not on Asgard. Surtur plans to unite his crown with the Eternal Flame so that he can cause Ragnarok, though Thor manages to defeat him and escape with his crown. During his battle with Hela however, Thor realizes causing Ragnarok is the only way he can defeat her, so he tasks Loki with resurrecting Surtur with the Eternal Flame, allowing Surtur to succeed in his plans and kill Hela while Thor, Loki, and the Asgardians escape.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Thor: Ragnarok*. Alternate universe variants of Surtur appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10)

## T

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=155 "Edit section: T")]

### Talos

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=156 "Edit section: Talos")]

See also: [Talos the Untamed](/wiki/Talos_the_Untamed "Talos the Untamed")

**Talos** (portrayed by [Ben Mendelsohn](/wiki/Ben_Mendelsohn "Ben Mendelsohn")) is a Skrull who is initially believed to be a terrorist and comes into conflict with [Carol Danvers](/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Nick Fury](/wiki/Nick_Fury_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), while disguised as agent **R. Keller**. However, Danvers agrees to help him and the Skrulls find a new home after Talos revealed the Kree destroyed their homeworld and have driven them to near-extinction. When Danvers struggles to locate a new home world for the Skrulls, Fury allows Talos and a faction of Skrulls to live on Earth, blend in with humanity, and assist him in protecting the planet while he continues looking for a permanent home for them.

In 2018, Talos survives [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip") and learns that the Skrulls are still being hunted across the universe by enemy forces. With no other options, Talos allows a million surviving Skrulls to move onto Earth for the meantime.

In 2024, following the resurrection of the Blip victims, he assumes Fury's role on Earth while Fury works in space and alongside his wife Soren (who assumes [Maria Hill](/wiki/Maria_Hill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Maria Hill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s role) assists [Spider-Man](/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and Mysterio in Europe in fighting the Elementals, eventually discovering the latter's fraudulence and his fabrication of the creatures.

In 2026, Talos is kicked off the Skrulls' governmental council and replaced by the radicalized Gravik, who kills Soren and recruits an unaware G'iah to his cause, planning to trick humanity into waging war on each other so that Skrulls can conquer Earth as revenge for Fury's broken promises. Talos contacts Hill and Fury to help him stop Gravik and find a way for humans and Skrulls to peacefully coexist on Earth. Talos works with them to try to prevent a bombing in Russia, however they are unable to stop it and Hill is killed by Gravik. He and Fury continue to work together and prevent an attack on a [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") plane and save U.S. President Ritson from an assassination attempt. However, Talos is killed by Gravik, who disguised himself as an ally soldier.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Captain Marvel* and *Spider-Man: Far From Home*; and the Disney+ series *Secret Invasion*.

### Taserface

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=157 "Edit section: Taserface")]

See also: [Taserface](/wiki/Taserface "Taserface")

**Taserface** (portrayed by [Chris Sullivan](/wiki/Chris_Sullivan_(actor) "Chris Sullivan (actor)")) was a Ravager mercenary and a lieutenant in Yondu Udonta's Ravager Clan. He is depicted as being proud of his name as he believes it strikes fear into the hearts of his enemies. However, [Rocket](/wiki/Rocket_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and the other Ravagers scoff at the ridiculousness of his name. Following Yondu's exile by Stakar Ogord, Taserface leads a mutiny against Yondu, feeling that he is "going soft", and kills anyone still loyal to him. After Kraglin aids Yondu, Rocket, and [Groot](/wiki/Groot_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in escaping from their prison cells, Yondu kills the remaining Ravagers and destroys the main engine, causing the Ravager ship to explode. While the heroes eject from the main ship in a smaller escape ship, Taserface contacts the [Sovereign](/wiki/Sovereign_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sovereign (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to give them Yondu's coordinates before dying in the explosion.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2*. Alternate universe variants of Taserface appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10): 2

### Taweret

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=158 "Edit section: Taweret")]

See also: [Taweret (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Taweret_(Marvel_Comics) "Taweret (Marvel Comics)")

**Taweret** (voiced and motion-captured by Antonia Salib) is the [hippopotamus](/wiki/Hippopotamus "Hippopotamus")-headed Egyptian goddess of childbirth and fertility. She helps guide [Marc Spector and Steven Grant](#Marc_Spector_/_Moon_Knight) through the [Duat](/wiki/Duat "Duat"). [Layla El-Faouly](/wiki/Layla_El-Faouly "Layla El-Faouly") temporarily becomes Taweret's avatar to help Spector and Grant in fighting [Arthur Harrow](/wiki/Arthur_Harrow "Arthur Harrow"). She is based on the [Egyptian goddess of the same name](/wiki/Taweret "Taweret").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Moon Knight*.

### Leila Taylor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=159 "Edit section: Leila Taylor")]

See also: [Leila Taylor (comics)](/wiki/Leila_Taylor_(comics) "Leila Taylor (comics)")

**Leila Taylor** (portrayed by [Xosha Roquemore](/wiki/Xosha_Roquemore "Xosha Roquemore")) is a [Secret Service](/wiki/United_States_Secret_Service "United States Secret Service") agent working for President Ross who has a history with Wilson. Taylor is a romantic interest for Wilson in the comics, but the film does not indicate any romantic interest between the characters.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Captain America: Brave New World*.

### T'Chaka / Black Panther

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=160 "Edit section: T'Chaka / Black Panther")]

See also: [T'Chaka](/wiki/T%27Chaka "T'Chaka")

**T'Chaka** (portrayed by [John Kani](/wiki/John_Kani "John Kani") as an elderly man and [Atandwa Kani](/wiki/Atandwa_Kani "Atandwa Kani") as a young man) is the former King of Wakanda. In 1992, T'Chaka learned his brother N'Jobu was plotting acts of open insurrection with plans to have Wakanda take a more aggressive foreign policy to fight the social injustice he witnessed in his assigned country. T'Chaka confronts N'Jobu and when N'Jobu attacked, T'Chaka kills him in defense of [Zuri](/wiki/Zuri_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Zuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), who was acting as a spy. Distressed at this act and concerned about maintaining Wakanda's security above all else, T'Chaka chooses to promptly return to his nation and leaves his nephew, N'Jadaka, abandoned as a child orphan. In 2016, during a meeting ratifying the Sokovian Accords at the Vienna International Center, T'Chaka is killed by an explosion. The Winter Soldier is originally believed to be behind the attack, but it is later discovered that he was framed by [Helmut Zemo](#Helmut_Zemo).[[171]](#cite_note-CastListFansided-171) Following this, T'Challa later learns the truth about N'Jobu and the resulting cover-up by his late father. While visiting the ancestor lands, T'Challa tells T'Chaka and the previous Black Panthers before him that he will lead Wakanda in a manner differently to them for the purpose of correcting his father's past mistakes.[[172]](#cite_note-172)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the films *Captain America: Civil War* and *Black Panther*. Alternate universe variants of T'Chaka appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10): 2

### T'Challa / Black Panther

Prince **T'Challa II** (portrayed by Divine Love Konadu-Sun), legally known as **Toussaint**, is the son of [Nakia](/wiki/Nakia_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Nakia (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and the late King [T'Challa](/wiki/T%27Challa_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") of Wakanda.

### Ted / Man-Thing

See also: [Man-Thing](/wiki/Man-Thing "Man-Thing")

**Ted** (motion-captured by Carey Jones, vocal effects provided by [Jeffrey Ford](/wiki/Jeffrey_Ford_(film_editor) "Jeffrey Ford (film editor)")), also known as **Man-Thing**, is a [swamp monster](/wiki/List_of_swamp_monsters "List of swamp monsters") and friend of [Jack Russell](/wiki/Jack_Russell_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Jack Russell (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who was captured by the Bloodstone family for their hunt. He was rescued by Jack Russell when he became Werewolf by Night.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ special *Werewolf by Night*.[[173]](#cite_note-173)[[174]](#cite_note-174)

### Thena

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=165 "Edit section: Thena")]

See also: [Thena](/wiki/Thena "Thena")

**Thena** (portrayed by [Angelina Jolie](/wiki/Angelina_Jolie "Angelina Jolie")) is a fierce warrior Eternal who can form any weapon out of cosmic energy. Because of her [Mahd Wy'ry](/wiki/Mahd_Wy%27ry "Mahd Wy'ry") "illness", [Gilgamesh](/wiki/Gilgamesh_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Gilgamesh (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") becomes her guardian over the centuries.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Eternals*.[[2]](#cite_note-EternalsEWAug2021-2)

### Flash Thompson

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=166 "Edit section: Flash Thompson")]

See also: [Flash Thompson](/wiki/Flash_Thompson "Flash Thompson")

**Eugene "Flash" Thompson** (portrayed by [Tony Revolori](/wiki/Tony_Revolori "Tony Revolori")) is a student at the Midtown School of Science and Technology. He is generally depicted as a school bully who often torments [Peter Parker](/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") but admires Spider-Man, unaware that the two are the same person. In 2018, he is a victim of [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip"). In 2023, he is revived to life. In 2024, he attends a trip to Europe and is a social media personality, with followers he dubs the "Flash Mob". After the trip, Flash's difficult home life is alluded to, as his father is ill and his mother is distant, having sent a chauffeur to pick him up from the airport rather than do so herself.[[175]](#cite_note-175) Shortly after the trip, he learns Parker is Spider-Man when [Quentin Beck](/wiki/Quentin_Beck_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Quentin Beck (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") reveals it to the world and is among his supporters after Parker is incriminated for Beck's murder, seemingly having written a book called "Flashpoint" describing his and Parker's "friendship". He is soon admitted into [MIT](/wiki/Massachusetts_Institute_of_Technology "Massachusetts Institute of Technology") and later directs Parker to an MIT administrator so the latter can help get [Ned Leeds](/wiki/Ned_Leeds_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Michelle Jones](/wiki/MJ_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "MJ (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") a second chance in.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *Spider-Man: Homecoming*, *Spider-Man: Far From Home*, and *Spider-Man: No Way Home*; and the web series *[The Daily Bugle](/wiki/The_Daily_Bugle_(web_series) "The Daily Bugle (web series)")* (2019–2022).[[176]](#cite_note-176)

### Taneleer Tivan / Collector

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=167 "Edit section: Taneleer Tivan / Collector")]

See also: [Collector (character)](/wiki/Collector_(character) "Collector (character)")

**Taneleer Tivan** (portrayed by [Benicio del Toro](/wiki/Benicio_del_Toro "Benicio del Toro")), also known as the **Collector**, is the renowned keeper of the largest collection of interstellar fauna, relics and species in the galaxy, operating from the Knowhere port installation. He is the [Grandmaster](/wiki/Grandmaster_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Grandmaster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s brother. In 2013, [Sif](#Sif) and [Volstagg](#Volstagg) bring the [Reality Stone](/wiki/Reality_Stone "Reality Stone") to Tivan for safekeeping, citing the foolishness of storing both the Reality Stone and the Space Stone in the same place. In 2014, he meets the [Guardians of the Galaxy](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and is nearly able to obtain the Power Stone. In 2018, he is confronted by [Thanos](/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), who takes the Reality Stone and destroys his collection. By 2025, Tivan sells the damaged Knowhere to the Guardians of the Galaxy.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Thor: The Dark World* (mid-credits cameo), *Guardians of the Galaxy*, and *Avengers: Infinity War*. Alternate universe variants of Tivan appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*;[[10]](#cite_note-WhatIfProductionBrief2-10) and the mixed reality experience *What If...? – An Immersive Story* (voiced by Stephen Alcalá).[[134]](#cite_note-ImmersiveStoryCast-134)[[133]](#cite_note-ImmersiveStoryGizmodo-133)

### Adrian Toomes / Vulture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=168 "Edit section: Adrian Toomes / Vulture")]

See also: [Vulture (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Vulture_(Marvel_Comics) "Vulture (Marvel Comics)")

**Adrian Toomes** (portrayed by [Michael Keaton](/wiki/Michael_Keaton "Michael Keaton")), also known as the **Vulture**, is the former owner of Bestman Salvage. In 2012, he chooses to become a criminal after the creation of the Department of Damage Control, a joint venture between the federal government and Tony Stark following the Battle of New York, which results in Toomes's company being run out of business. With his associates, [Phineas Mason](#Phineas_Mason), [Herman Schultz](#Herman_Schultz_/_Shocker), [Jackson Brice](/wiki/Jackson_Brice_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Jackson Brice (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and [Randy Vale](/wiki/Randy_Vale_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Randy Vale (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), he begins an illegal arms-dealing business that reverse-engineers and weaponizes Chitauri technology that they have scavenged and salvaged, and sells it on the black market. He is also the father of [Liz](/wiki/Liz_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Liz (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Toomes's flight suit is equipped with turbine-powered wings, claw-like wingtips, and boot-mounted talons.

In 2016, he comes into conflict with Spider-Man and, after deducing his secret identity as Peter Parker, Toomes threatens him with retaliation unless he stops interfering. However, Parker thwarts Toomes's attempt to hijack a plane carrying Avengers weaponry and saves his life when his suit malfunctions before Happy Hogan and the FBI find and arrest Toomes. As a result, his family moves away. Later, an imprisoned Toomes is approached by [Mac Gargan](/wiki/Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_A%E2%80%93L#Mac_Gargan "Characters of the Marvel Cinematic Universe: A–L"), who wants to confirm whether he knows Spider-Man's identity, which Toomes falsely denies. In 2024, Toomes is transported to another universe and released without criminal charges. He builds a new Vulture suit and approaches [Michael Morbius](/wiki/Morbius_(Sony%27s_Spider-Man_Universe) "Morbius (Sony's Spider-Man Universe)") in an effort to form a team.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Spider-Man: Homecoming*.[[177]](#cite_note-177) Keaton reprises his role in the [Sony's Spider-Man Universe](/wiki/Sony%27s_Spider-Man_Universe "Sony's Spider-Man Universe") (SSU) film *[Morbius](/wiki/Morbius_(film) "Morbius (film)")* (2022) (mid-credits cameo).[[178]](#cite_note-178)

### Topaz

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=169 "Edit section: Topaz")]

See also: [Topaz (Ultraforce)](/wiki/Topaz_(Ultraforce) "Topaz (Ultraforce)")

**Topaz** (portrayed by [Rachel House](/wiki/Rachel_House_(actress) "Rachel House (actress)")) is the Grandmaster's personal bodyguard and head of the Sakaaran Guards who had assisted him in overlooking the prisoners involved with the Contest of Champions. When a riot had broken out involving several former prisoners, as well as Thor and Valkyrie, Topaz led her soldiers in fighting back, only to eventually lose her life while chasing Bruce Banner.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Thor: Ragnarok*. An alternate universe variant of Topaz appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.

### Joaquin Torres / Falcon

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=170 "Edit section: Joaquin Torres / Falcon")]

See also: [Joaquin Torres (comics)](/wiki/Joaquin_Torres_(comics) "Joaquin Torres (comics)")

**Joaquin Torres** (portrayed by [Danny Ramirez](/wiki/Danny_Ramirez "Danny Ramirez")), also known as the **Falcon**, is a member of the U.S. Air Force and best friends with [Sam Wilson](/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Born in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") and raised in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami"), [Florida](/wiki/Florida "Florida") since the age of six.[[179]](#cite_note-179) He survived [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip") and tells Wilson of the five years he missed. He informs him of the terrorist Flag Smashers and provides intel on them, as well as meeting Wilson's other friend, [Bucky Barnes](/wiki/Bucky_Barnes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). He is passed on the [EXO-7 Falcon](/wiki/EXO-7_Falcon "EXO-7 Falcon") wingsuit and mantle by Wilson, when the latter takes on the mantle of Captain America.

Wilson and Torres successfully intercept an illegal sale of [adamantium](/wiki/Adamantium "Adamantium") in [Oaxaca](/wiki/Oaxaca "Oaxaca"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). As a reward, Wilson introduces Torres to [Isaiah Bradley](/wiki/Isaiah_Bradley_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Isaiah Bradley (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). After [President](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") [Thaddeus Ross](/wiki/Thaddeus_Ross_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Thaddeus Ross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") is attacked at the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House"), Wilson and Torres investigate a hidden site in West Virginia called Camp Echo One. Later on they discover that [Samuel Sterns](/wiki/Samuel_Sterns_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Samuel Sterns (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") used mind control on Bradley to attack Ross. At [Celestial Island](/wiki/Tiamut "Tiamut") on the [Indian Ocean](/wiki/Indian_Ocean "Indian Ocean"), Wilson and Torres stop two rogue pilots from attacking the Japanese fleet to prevent a war between Japan and the U.S. Although successful, Torres is injured. While recovering, Torres is asked by Wilson to join the Avengers.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the Disney+ series *[The Falcon and the Winter Soldier](/wiki/The_Falcon_and_the_Winter_Soldier "The Falcon and the Winter Soldier")*; and the film *[Captain America: Brave New World](/wiki/Captain_America:_Brave_New_World "Captain America: Brave New World")*.[[180]](#cite_note-180)[[181]](#cite_note-181)[[182]](#cite_note-182) He will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29)

## U

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=171 "Edit section: U")]

### Uatu / Watcher

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=172 "Edit section: Uatu / Watcher")]

Main article: [Watcher (Marvel Cinematic Universe)](/wiki/Watcher_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Watcher (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")

### Yondu Udonta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=173 "Edit section: Yondu Udonta")]

See also: [Yondu](/wiki/Yondu "Yondu")

**Yondu Udonta** (portrayed by [Michael Rooker](/wiki/Michael_Rooker "Michael Rooker")) is the adoptive father of Peter Quill and a leader of the Ravagers. Yondu had been hired to kidnap a young Quill from Earth after the death of Quill's mother and return him to his birth father, Ego. Yondu decided Quill would better serve his own needs in thievery, so he raised him as part of his Ravager clan. Despite their frequent arguments and conflicts over the years, Quill and Yondu form an emotional and familial bond. In 2014, Yondu helps Quill and the Guardians of the Galaxy on Xandar against Ronan, and months later, sacrifices himself to save Quill from Ego.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *Guardians of the Galaxy*, *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2*, and *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3* (cameo); and the Disney+ special *The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special*. Alternate universe variants of Yondu appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[67]](#cite_note-WhatIfSDCC-67)

### Ultron

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=174 "Edit section: Ultron")]

See also: [Ultron](/wiki/Ultron "Ultron")

**Ultron** (portrayed by [James Spader](/wiki/James_Spader "James Spader")) is an artificial intelligence conceived and designed by Tony Stark and Bruce Banner as the head of a peacekeeping program, who subsequently takes the form of a sentient android overwhelmed with a [god complex](/wiki/God_complex "God complex") and a strong misanthropy, deciding to protect the Earth by eradicating humanity, considering them a harmful threat for the Earth.[[183]](#cite_note-Breznican-2014-183) Stark and Banner's research on the Mind Stone are the groundwork for Ultron's genesis, along with Stark's belief that Ultron would be the Avengers solution to maintaining "[peace in our time](/wiki/Peace_for_our_time "Peace for our time")." Once born, Ultron, however, being activated and infected by the Mind Stone, quickly surmises that humanity's continued survival throughout human history is a result of overcoming ongoing successions of crises, and thus, he determines to inflict an [extinction-level event](/wiki/Extinction_event "Extinction event") in Sokovia to ensure the people of Earth have the will to evolve.[[183]](#cite_note-Breznican-2014-183) Ultron, however, views the Avengers as a hindrance to humanity's evolution and vows to fulfill the Avengers' extinction. Ultron continuously updates his physical form with the acquisition of vibranium from Ulysses Klaue. He also possesses the ability to manifest himself within Stark's Iron Legion droids and the Ultron Sentries he constructs soon after. He is ultimately defeated by the Avengers and destroyed by [Vision](/wiki/Vision_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), whom he intended to be his final prime body.

In [an alternate universe](/wiki/What_If..._Ultron_Won%3F "What If... Ultron Won?"), Ultron (voiced by [Ross Marquand](/wiki/Ross_Marquand "Ross Marquand")) transfers his A.I. into the not-yet born Vision's body, defeats the Avengers, and eradicates most of Earth's lifeforms. He then kills Thanos and takes the Infinity Stones, using them to eradicate all other life in the universe. Ultron also becomes aware of the [Watcher](#Watcher), and manages to break into the Nexus of All Realities in an attempt to kill him and destroy the multiverse. After the Watcher forms the [Guardians of the Multiverse](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Multiverse "Guardians of the Multiverse"), Ultron becomes aware of and battles the team, but fails to defeat them due to [Stephen Strange Supreme](/wiki/Doctor_Strange_Supreme "Doctor Strange Supreme")'s immense power and protection spells. Natasha Romanoff and [Captain Peggy Carter](/wiki/Captain_Carter "Captain Carter") manage to shoot an arrow into his head which contains a copy of Arnim Zola's consciousness. Subsequently, Zola destroys Ultron from inside his body and takes over it, but starts fighting Killmonger over the Infinity Stones until both are trapped in a pocket dimension by Strange Supreme and the Watcher. After going insane, Strange has Killmonger, Zola and a time displaced Ultron amongst his prisoners, but they are returned to their own universes by Kahhori after she strips Killmonger of the Infinity Stones.

In an alternate universe observed by the Watcher, Ultron was programmed to sing show tunes.

In another alternate universe, a variant of Ultron with a similar backstory to the one previously fought by the Guardians of the Multiverse destroys his universe, but never hears the Watcher and instead stays in his destroyed universe for eons. In order to rescue the Watcher, Captain Carter, [Kahhori](/wiki/Kahhori "Kahhori"), Byrdie the Duck and Storm seek out his help. Having had eons in an empty universe to reconsider his actions, this Ultron expresses remorse for what he did and agrees to help save both the kidnapped Carter and the Watcher. Ultron helps to rescue the pair and stays behind, sacrificing himself so that the Guardians can escape. Even with the full power of the Infinity Stones on his side, Ultron proves to be no match for the Watchers and he is quickly destroyed by the Eminence. However, his sacrifice allows the Guardians time to come up with a plan to face the Watchers.

On Earth-838, Ultron Sentries (also voiced by Marquand) serve as guards working for the Illuminati that apprehend Stephen Strange and America Chavez when they arrive there. Many of them are later destroyed by Wanda Maximoff.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Avengers: Age of Ultron*.[[184]](#cite_note-184) He will return in the upcoming Disney+ series *VisionQuest*.[[185]](#cite_note-185) Alternate universe variants of Ultron appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*; and the film *Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness*.[[186]](#cite_note-WhatIfTrailerBreakdown-186)[[146]](#cite_note-WI7EEs-146)

### BB Urich

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=175 "Edit section: BB Urich")]

**BB Urich** (portrayed by [Genneya Walton](/wiki/Genneya_Walton "Genneya Walton"))[[187]](#cite_note-187) is a journalist for *The BB Report* and the niece of [Ben Urich](/wiki/Ben_Urich_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ben Urich (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Daredevil: Born Again*.

## V

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=177 "Edit section: V")]

### Valkyrie

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=178 "Edit section: Valkyrie")]

See also: [Valkyrie (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Valkyrie_(Marvel_Comics) "Valkyrie (Marvel Comics)")

**King** "**Val**" **Valkyrie** (portrayed by [Tessa Thompson](/wiki/Tessa_Thompson "Tessa Thompson")), also known as **Scrapper 142**, is the last surviving of a group of Asgardian female warriors called the Valkyries, who were all, save Scrapper, vanquished in battle against Hela. In 2017, she allies herself with Thor and Bruce Banner to fight Hela once more. She survives the Blip and brings the remaining Asgardians to a town in Norway called New Asgard, which she *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* rules in place of the depressed Thor by 2023. She later joins the Avengers in their final battle against Thanos and his army. After the battle, Thor officially leaves her in charge of New Asgard as its leader, and she later assists him in facing Gorr the God-Butcher, acquiring Zeus's Thunderbolt for herself in the process. Later, she aids Carol Danvers in supplying refuge to Emperor
Droge and his Skrull colony.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *Thor: Ragnarok*, *Avengers: Endgame*, *Thor: Love and Thunder*, and *The Marvels*. Alternate universe variants of Valkyrie appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* and *Marvel Zombies*.[[146]](#cite_note-WI7EEs-146)[[188]](#cite_note-MarvelZombiesCast2-188)

### Hope van Dyne / Wasp

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=179 "Edit section: Hope van Dyne / Wasp")]

Main article: [Hope van Dyne](/wiki/Hope_van_Dyne "Hope van Dyne")

### Janet van Dyne / Wasp

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=180 "Edit section: Janet van Dyne / Wasp")]

See also: [Wasp (character)](/wiki/Wasp_(character) "Wasp (character)")

**Janet van Dyne** (portrayed by [Michelle Pfeiffer](/wiki/Michelle_Pfeiffer "Michelle Pfeiffer")) is a scientist, the wife of [Hank Pym](/wiki/Hank_Pym_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), mother of [Hope van Dyne](/wiki/Hope_van_Dyne_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Hope van Dyne (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), and the MCU's original **Wasp**. As the Wasp, Janet operated as high-ranking agent at [S.H.I.E.L.D.](/wiki/S.H.I.E.L.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") alongside Pym on field missions where she wore a shrinking suit with similar powers as the Ant-Man one, with the added capability of wings for flight. During a mission in 1987, Janet turned off her suit regulator and became sub-atomic, disappearing into the [Quantum Realm](/wiki/Quantum_Realm "Quantum Realm"). During this time, she meets [Kang the Conqueror](/wiki/Kang_the_Conqueror_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Kang the Conqueror (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") who had been exiled there. She helps him fix his Time Chair and restore his [multiversal](/wiki/Multiverse_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Multiverse (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") power core. However, she learns through a neurolink with the chair of Kang's true intentions of destruction. She betrays him and makes the core unusable, before running away and joining a refugee resistance movement. In 2018, she is rescued by Pym with Hope and [Scott Lang](/wiki/Scott_Lang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s help. Shortly after, Janet becomes a victim of [the Blip](/wiki/The_Blip "The Blip"). In 2023, she is restored to life and attends [Tony Stark](/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s funeral with Pym, Hope, and Lang. In 2025, she, Pym, Hope, Lang, and [Cassie Lang](/wiki/Cassie_Lang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Cassie Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") are accidentally transported to the Quantum Realm where she encounters Kang again and Lang and Hope seemingly keep him from escaping.

In an [alternate universe](/wiki/What_If..._Zombies%3F! "What If... Zombies?!"), Janet is [patient zero](/wiki/Index_case "Index case") for an infectious quantum virus that has turned her into a [zombie](/wiki/Zombie "Zombie"). After being rescued by Pym from the Quantum Realm in 2018, the virus is spread across the world. She later takes part of the Red Queen's army during the battle for Infinity Hulk, in which she is killed by Valkyrie.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in four projects: the films *Ant-Man* (flashback), *Ant-Man and the Wasp*, *Avengers: Endgame*, and *Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania*. An alternate universe variant of van Dyne appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* and *Marvel Zombies*.[[93]](#cite_note-TrailerCharactersMZ-93)

### Anton Vanko

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=181 "Edit section: Anton Vanko")]

See also: [Anton Vanko](/wiki/Anton_Vanko "Anton Vanko")

**Anton Vanko** (portrayed by [Yevgeni Lazarev](/wiki/Yevgeni_Lazarev "Yevgeni Lazarev") as an elderly man and [Costa Ronin](/wiki/Costa_Ronin "Costa Ronin") as a young man) is a Soviet scientist and partner of Howard Stark who both help create the first arc reactor. He betrays Stark by selling their designs on the [black market](/wiki/Black_market "Black market"). When Stark finds out, Vanko is deported and becomes a drunk. His son [Ivan Vanko](#Ivan_Vanko_/_Whiplash) vows revenge on the Stark family after his death.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the film *Iron Man 2*; and the Marvel Television series *Agent Carter*.[[189]](#cite_note-189)

### Ivan Vanko / Whiplash

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=182 "Edit section: Ivan Vanko / Whiplash")]

See also: [Whiplash (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Whiplash_(Marvel_Comics) "Whiplash (Marvel Comics)")

**Ivan Vanko** (portrayed by [Mickey Rourke](/wiki/Mickey_Rourke "Mickey Rourke")), also known as **Whiplash**, is the son of [Anton Vanko](#Anton_Vanko). Learning what he knew of his father of the original model of the arc reactor, he uses it to create electric whips to kill Tony Stark. After his failure to do so, he attracts business rival [Justin Hammer](/wiki/Justin_Hammer_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Justin Hammer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), for the creation of a new suit for Hammer, although Vanko later betrays him using drones to attack Stark and then attempts to kill him with a new exoskeleton suit. He is defeated in battle with the combined forces of Stark and James Rhodes and subsequently killed by his exoskeleton suit self-destructing.[[190]](#cite_note-190)[[139]](#cite_note-villain_1-139)[[141]](#cite_note-villain_2-141)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Iron Man 2*.

### Varra / Priscilla Davis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=183 "Edit section: Varra / Priscilla Davis")]

See also: [Varra (comics)](/wiki/Varra_(comics) "Varra (comics)")

**Varra** (portrayed by [Charlayne Woodard](/wiki/Charlayne_Woodard "Charlayne Woodard")) is one of the many Skrull refugees on Earth. She met and fell in love with Nick Fury, marrying him. She is also shown to have a history with Gravik. Varra posed as **Priscilla Davis** who had a heart defect and was buried at sea. Varra could not bring herself to carry out Gravik's orders to kill Fury.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *Secret Invasion*.

### Seth Voelker / Sidewinder

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=185 "Edit section: Seth Voelker / Sidewinder")]

See also: [Sidewinder (character)](/wiki/Sidewinder_(character) "Sidewinder (character)")

**Seth Voelker** (portrayed by [Giancarlo Esposito](/wiki/Giancarlo_Esposito "Giancarlo Esposito")), also known as **Sidewinder**, is the leader of the violent [special ops](/wiki/Special_ops "Special ops") team [Serpent](/wiki/Serpent_Society_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Serpent Society (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project, the film *Captain America: Brave New World*.[[191]](#cite_note-191)

### Volstagg

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=186 "Edit section: Volstagg")]

See also: [Volstagg](/wiki/Volstagg "Volstagg")

**Volstagg** (portrayed by [Ray Stevenson](/wiki/Ray_Stevenson "Ray Stevenson")) is a member of the Warriors Three, depicted as a warrior of Asgard who loves to eat. He is killed by Hela in 2017 when she invades Asgard after being freed from her imprisonment.[[192]](#cite_note-Dashing-192)[[193]](#cite_note-W3_Fate-193)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in three projects: the films *Thor*, *Thor: The Dark World*, and *Thor: Ragnarok*. An alternate universe variant of Volstagg (voiced by [Fred Tatasciore](/wiki/Fred_Tatasciore "Fred Tatasciore")) appeared in one project: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*.[[194]](#cite_note-WI7Cast-194)

### Victor von Doom / Doctor Doom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=187 "Edit section: Victor von Doom / Doctor Doom")]

See also: [Doctor Doom](/wiki/Doctor_Doom "Doctor Doom")

**Victor von Doom** (portrayed by [Robert Downey Jr.](/wiki/Robert_Downey_Jr. "Robert Downey Jr.")), also known as **Doctor Doom**, is the despotic ruler of the Eastern European nation of [Latveria](/wiki/Latveria_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Latveria (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *The Fantastic Four: First Steps* (mid-credits cameo).[[195]](#cite_note-DoomFirstSteps-195) He will return in the films *Avengers: Doomsday* and *Avengers: Secret Wars*.[[196]](#cite_note-196)

### Melina Vostokoff

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=188 "Edit section: Melina Vostokoff")]

See also: [Iron Maiden (Marvel Comics)](/wiki/Iron_Maiden_(Marvel_Comics) "Iron Maiden (Marvel Comics)")

**Melina Vostokoff** (portrayed by [Rachel Weisz](/wiki/Rachel_Weisz "Rachel Weisz")) is a seasoned spy who trained in the [Red Room](/wiki/Red_Room_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Red Room (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") as a Black Widow and is a mother-figure to Natasha Romanoff and Yelena Belova. She is also a scientist who does research into mind control methods for General [Dreykov](/wiki/Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_A%E2%80%93L#Dreykov "Characters of the Marvel Cinematic Universe: A–L"). Melina later helps Romanoff to rescue the Widows and destroy the Red Room.

In an alternate universe depicted in the Disney+ animated series *What If...?*, Vostokoff became the leader of the Red Room after Romanoff had killed the previous leader and she acts as the handler of a brainwashed [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#What_If...?_(2021–2024) "Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), targeting Romanoff and [Captain Carter](/wiki/Peggy_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)#What_If...? "Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"). Carter manages to break through Rogers's brainwashing, and he seemingly sacrifices himself to destroy the Red Room. As Rogers flies the Hydra Stomper armor into the facility, Romanoff attaches Vostokoff to the armor via grappling hook, leading to her demise in the explosion.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Black Widow*. Alternate universe variants of Vostokoff appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* (voiced by Weisz in the second season and [Kari Wahlgren](/wiki/Kari_Wahlgren "Kari Wahlgren") in the third season) and *Marvel Zombies* (voiced by Wahlgren).

## W

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=189 "Edit section: W")]

### John Walker / Captain America / U.S. Agent

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=190 "Edit section: John Walker / Captain America / U.S. Agent")]

See also: [U.S. Agent](/wiki/U.S._Agent "U.S. Agent")

After the retirement and disappearance of [Steve Rogers](/wiki/Steve_Rogers "Steve Rogers"), the [U.S. Government](/wiki/U.S._Government "U.S. Government") selected and approved [Captain](/wiki/Captain_(United_States_O-3) "Captain (United States O-3)") **John F. Walker** (portrayed by [Wyatt Russell](/wiki/Wyatt_Russell "Wyatt Russell")) as the second **Captain America**. The government also approved Walker's long-time friend and fellow [Operation Enduring Freedom](/wiki/Operation_Enduring_Freedom "Operation Enduring Freedom") veteran, [Lemar Hoskins](/wiki/Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_A%E2%80%93L#Lemar_Hoskins_/_Battlestar "Characters of the Marvel Cinematic Universe: A–L"), as Captain America's new partner, code-named "Battlestar". Walker was a [football](/wiki/American_football "American football") star at Custer's Grove High School in [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)") and graduated from [West Point](/wiki/United_States_Military_Academy "United States Military Academy") in 2009. He went on to become a highly decorated [U.S. Army](/wiki/U.S._Army "U.S. Army") Captain in the [75th Ranger Regiment](/wiki/75th_Ranger_Regiment "75th Ranger Regiment") and the first person in history to receive the [Medal of Honor](/wiki/Medal_of_Honor "Medal of Honor") three times for his combat service. He also commanded high level [counter-terrorism](/wiki/Counter-terrorism "Counter-terrorism") and hostage rescue operations. His body was studied at [MIT](/wiki/MIT "MIT") and tested well above average in speed, endurance, and intelligence.

The [Global Repatriation Council](/wiki/Global_Repatriation_Council "Global Repatriation Council") (GRC) tasked Walker and Hoskins to help quash the ongoing violent post-Blip revolutions occurring across the world. He comes to the aid of [Sam Wilson](/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") and [Bucky Barnes](/wiki/Bucky_Barnes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") during their first confrontation with the Flag Smashers and attempts to recruit them to join the GRC but they refuse. Walker later assists Barnes after he is arrested for missing court-mandated therapy and again is refused when he asks Barnes and Wilson to join him. Walker ultimately warns them to stay out of his way. During a fight with the Flag Smashers, Walker retrieves a vial of Super Soldier serum, and decides to ingest it. In the ensuing battle with the Flag Smashers, [Karli Morgenthau](#Karli_Morgenthau) kills Hoskins during her attempt to kill Walker. In pursuit, Walker kills one of her accomplices by driving the shield into the man's chest. This proves controversial, as eyewitnesses capturing the events on social media describe it as murder, while Walker maintains that he upheld his mandate and the [rules of engagement](/wiki/Rules_of_Engagement "Rules of Engagement") for using lethal force on a terrorist.

Over this incident, Wilson and Barnes elect to fight him for the shield and defeat him. The GRC and U.S. Government opted to sanction Walker severely in order to quell the media scandal; in doing so, they strip Walker of his role as Captain America, and he is [other than honorably discharged](/wiki/Other_than_honorable_discharge "Other than honorable discharge") from the army. In spite of this, Walker publicly maintains his defense that he was following his orders in accordance with prior pursuits of terrorists and in a manner consistent with his mandate. Once discharged, he builds a new shield from scrap metal and his [Medal of Honor](/wiki/Medal_of_Honor "Medal of Honor"). Thus equipped, Walker confronts the Flag Smashers to avenge Hoskins, but defers his original goal and saves the Flag Smashers' hostages. After Wilson helps save them as Captain America, Walker assists Barnes in capturing the Flag Smashers. Afterwards, Contessa [Valentina Allegra de Fontaine](/wiki/Valentina_Allegra_de_Fontaine_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") recruits Walker for clandestine work, stating that she needs a **U.S. Agent** and not Captain America.

Over the next several years, de Fontaine sends Walker on numerous [covert operations](/wiki/Covert_ops "Covert ops"). However, his public image of operating as Captain America remains disgraced in spite of his renewed service and his consumption of it has strained his marriage with Olivia. De Fontaine eventually assigns him to pursue and kill [Yelena Belova](/wiki/Yelena_Belova_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Yelena Belova (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") to prevent her from stealing information from a covert lab. After arriving and engaging Belova in combat, two additional covert agents, [Ava Starr](#Ava_Starr_/_Ghost) and [Antonia Dreykov](/wiki/Antonia_Dreykov "Antonia Dreykov") arrive and join the melee.

Walker and the others realize that they all work for de Fontaine and have similar orders to kill one another to cover up de Fontaine's illegal activities. Walker, Starr, Belova, and test subject ["Bob"](#Bob_Reynolds_/_Sentry_/_Void) escape the lab and a subsequent ambush by de Fontaine's soldiers, where Bob disappears after flying into the air. Regrouping, Walker, Belova, and Starr hitch a ride with [Alexei Shostakov](#Alexei_Shostakov_/_Red_Guardian), Belova's estranged adopted father and narrowly avoid capture through the intervention of Bucky Barnes. Realizing they have a common goal, the group attempts to arrest de Fontaine. Bob returns, now calling himself "The Sentry", and attacks the group on de Fontaine's orders. Walker defends his teammates throughout the fight, but Bob is too powerful, eventually folding Walker's shield in half and tearing off Bucky's cybernetic arm. Walker carries Bucky to safety and assists in saving civilians as Bob transforms from The Sentry into "The Void," the personification of his traumatized personality. While saving a civilian from falling debris, the others join Walker and regroup to confront The Void. Walker follows Belova into The Void's psychic construct and assists in rescuing Bob's core personality from the Void's control. Once returned to normal, Bob thanks the others for their assistance. Walker joins the others in attempting to arrest de Fontaine but discover that she has convened a press conference. Hailed as heroes, Walker becomes one of the "New Avengers", taking up residence in the former Avengers tower.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in two projects: the Disney+ series *The Falcon and the Winter Soldier* and the film *Thunderbolts\**.[[130]](#cite_note-Panaligan-2022-130) He will return in the film *Avengers: Doomsday*.[[29]](#cite_note-DoomsdayCastAnnoucement-29) Alternate universe variants of Walker appeared in two projects: the Disney+ animated series *What If...?* and *Marvel Zombies*.[[97]](#cite_note-WhatIfS3Cast-97)[[188]](#cite_note-MarvelZombiesCast2-188)

### Jennifer Walters / She-Hulk

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=191 "Edit section: Jennifer Walters / She-Hulk")]

See also: [She-Hulk](/wiki/She-Hulk "She-Hulk")

**Jennifer** "**Jen**" **Walters** (portrayed by [Tatiana Maslany](/wiki/Tatiana_Maslany "Tatiana Maslany")) is an attorney at Goodman, Lieber, Kurtzberg & Holliway and former Deputy District Attorney for the City of [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"). After coming into contact with her cousin [Bruce Banner](/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)")'s blood, she undergoes a physical transformation similar to his and becomes known as **She-Hulk**. She is then hired by GLK&H to be the face of their superhuman law division, and later dates [Matt Murdock](/wiki/Matt_Murdock_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Matt Murdock (Marvel Cinematic Universe)").

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the Disney+ series *She-Hulk: Attorney at Law.*

### Adam Warlock

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=193 "Edit section: Adam Warlock")]

See also: [Adam Warlock](/wiki/Adam_Warlock "Adam Warlock")

**Adam Warlock** (portrayed by [Will Poulter](/wiki/Will_Poulter "Will Poulter")) is a perfect [Sovereign](/wiki/Sovereign_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Sovereign (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") created by [Ayesha](/wiki/Ayesha_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Ayesha (Marvel Cinematic Universe)") in order to help her destroy the [Guardians of the Galaxy](/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe) "Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)"), but has the mentality of a child due to the High Evolutionary forcing Ayesha to bring Adam out prematurely to aid him in capturing Rocket. After the destruction of Counter-Earth, in which his mother is killed, Adam is captured by Gamora and later fails to kill Quill. As the High Evolutionary's ship explodes, Adam is rescued by Groot, and Drax explains that everyone deserves a second chance. Adam subsequently saves Quill's life and awkwardly joins the group hug, later becoming a part of the new Guardians of the Galaxy under the leadership of Rocket.

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3*.

**Natalie Washington** (portrayed by [Lyric Ross](/wiki/Lyric_Ross "Lyric Ross") as an adult and Kent Churchill as a child)[[197]](#cite_note-IronheartCharacterGuide2-197) is [Riri Williams](#Riri_Williams_/_Ironheart)'s late best friend that she turns into her in-suit [artificial intelligence](/wiki/Artificial_intelligence "Artificial intelligence") **N.A.T.A.L.I.E.**, short for **Neuro Autonomous Technical Assistant and Laboratory Intelligence Entity**.[[147]](#cite_note-IronheartCharacterGuide-147)

### Raza al-Wazar

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit&section=195 "Edit section: Raza al-Wazar")]

**Raza Hamidmi al-Wazar** (portrayed by [Faran Tahir](/wiki/Faran_Tahir "Faran Tahir")) is a terrorist leader of the Ten Rings group who kidnap Tony Stark. Outside of his character sharing some characteristics from the comic book character [Wong-Chu](/wiki/Wong-Chu "Wong-Chu") and referencing the organization that alludes to the [Mandarin](/wiki/Mandarin_(character) "Mandarin (character)"), Raza is an original character of the MCU.[[198]](#cite_note-Raza-198) He uses Stark Industries weapons for personal gain, and is responsible for the origin of Stark as Iron Man, as he is the one who abducted him for his self-proclaimed personal gain of taking over the world, citing [Genghis Khan](/wiki/Genghis_Khan "Genghis Khan") as his influence. He is later revealed to be working for Obadiah Stane, who seemingly kills him before disposing of his men.[[139]](#cite_note-villain_1-139)[[140]](#cite_note-hollywoodreporter.com-140)[[141]](#cite_note-villain_2-141)

As of 2026,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Characters_of_the_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe:_M%E2%80%93Z&action=edit) the character has appeared in one project: the film *Iron Man*. Raza will return in the Disney+ series *VisionQuest*.
